core.when: When implementing stock return percentage calculation
core.action: Use float type for each price-to-percentage conversions

consequence.description: Using float for monetary calculations in the backtest engine is an explicit domain design. All percentage conversions use float() (lines 172, 206, 394), not Decimal. Mixing types or using Decimal without consistent context would corrupt computed max_return, stock_return_pct, and simulated_return_pct fields.

core.when: When computing a global summary rollup over each stocks
core.action: Use OVERALL_SENTINEL_CODE='__overall__' as the code value for overall scope summaries

consequence.description: The overall summary uses OVERALL_SENTINEL_CODE='__overall__' as a sentinel code value (backtest_engine.py:15). Failing to use this sentinel would prevent the global rollup from being stored and retrieved correctly, breaking get_global_summary and all cross-stock performance reporting.

core.when: When configuring network connections in corporate or restricted network environments
core.action: Check USE_PROXY environment variable to determine proxy configuration — do not hardcode direct connections when proxy may be required

consequence.description: Hardcoded direct connections fail in corporate environments with mandatory proxy requirements, causing complete inability to fetch market data and backtest failure

core.when: When preparing data for ML model training in the framework
core.action: Implement temporal validation: verify each training samples have timestamps strictly before each test samples, and add assertion checks verifying no feature values in training set are derived from future data relative to the model evaluation period

consequence.description: Models trained on temporally leaked data produce artificially inflated backtest metrics; live trading returns will fall far below backtested performance, leading to strategy abandonment based on false signals

core.when: When fetching data from external providers
core.action: record data with timestamp greater than or equal to current time

consequence.description: Recording future timestamps introduces look-ahead bias, causing backtest results to be artificially inflated and unrepresentative of actual trading performance

core.when: When implementing trading cycle order logic
core.action: Execute sell orders before buy orders in each trading cycle

consequence.description: Backtest results will be incorrect because capital allocation would double-count positions that should be liquidated before new positions are opened

core.when: When using Entity.get for entity type resolution
core.action: expect Entity.get to return non-None for entity types not covered by _entity_to_endpoint

consequence.description: Entity types like BACKTEST, HEDGE, REPORT, SCENARIO are not in _entity_to_endpoint, causing Entity.get to fail with KeyError when accessing endpoint mapping

core.when: When implementing regression analysis in the timeseries module
core.action: Use statsmodels OLS for regression calculations to preserve full statistical inference capabilities (t-stats, R-squared, confidence intervals)

consequence.description: Replacing statsmodels OLS with numpy lstsq or sklearn LinearRegression strips statistical inference capabilities, making backtest results unreliable for hypothesis testing and confidence estimation

core.when: When computing bear market information ratio in performance attribution
core.action: Filter benchmark returns to periods where benchmark < 0 before computing excess returns and standard deviation; verify both numerator (excess return) and denominator (tracking error) use the same filtered bear market periods to avoid inconsistent metric computation

consequence.description: Computing excess returns over all periods but std only over bear periods produces an inconsistent information ratio that mixes filtered and unfiltered data, leading to incorrect performance attribution and potentially misleading drawdown analysis

core.when: When configuring data provider connections for multi-source data feeds
core.action: Implement provider priority configuration with explicit credential mapping per provider; use environment variables or secret manager for credential isolation rather than shared configuration

consequence.description: Without provider priority and credential isolation, primary provider failures cause cascading errors; fallback providers may fail with authentication errors, leading to data gaps in live trading

core.when: When marking accounts involved in alert patterns
core.action: set IS_SAR_KEY attribute to True for each vertices participating in alert typologies

consequence.description: Missing IS_SAR_KEY flag causes SAR account list generation to miss alerted accounts, breaking downstream compliance reporting requirements

core.when: When parsing SAR flag from input CSV files
core.action: Convert SAR flag to lowercase string 'true'/'false' for consistent CSV output

consequence.description: Alert filtering logic in downstream analytics fails silently because case-sensitive comparisons miss SAR transactions, causing compliance detection gaps

core.when: When converting transaction logs to CSV outputs
core.action: Output SAR flag values as lowercase string 'true' or 'false' (matching the schema specification)

consequence.description: Alert downstream processing systems expecting lowercase boolean strings may fail to correctly identify SAR-flagged transactions, causing incorrect compliance categorization

core.when: When filtering intraday data for US stocks
core.action: Filter out times outside NYSE trading hours (09:30 to 15:59) for intervals less than 1 day

consequence.description: Including pre-market or after-hours data in training without proper handling causes model to learn price patterns from non-liquid periods, degrading live trading performance

core.when: When performing rolling window retraining
core.action: Separate training, validation, and trading periods without overlap

consequence.description: Overlapping periods will cause look-ahead bias, making backtest results unrealistically optimistic compared to live trading

core.when: When executing paper trading mode via Alpaca API
core.action: validate API connection and test latency before initiating live orders

consequence.description: Network latency or API unavailability causes order submission delays, missed trading windows, and stale price fills resulting in execution slippage

core.when: When running ensemble strategy across multiple rebalancing windows
core.action: persist final portfolio state between windows as initial state for next iteration

consequence.description: Resetting portfolio state between windows causes discontinuous equity curve, incorrect cumulative returns, and broken PnL tracking across rebalancing periods

core.when: When selecting model for deterministic prediction
core.action: use deterministic=True parameter to verify reproducible trading actions

consequence.description: Non-deterministic policy introduces randomness in trade execution, causing inconsistent backtest results and making strategy performance unreproducible

core.when: When loading trained model for inference
core.action: validate model file exists and is compatible with DRL library

consequence.description: Failed model loading causes inference to crash, losing any partial results and requiring full restart of backtest

core.when: When deciding to skip latency testing before paper trading
core.action: skip API latency validation before initiating live order submission

consequence.description: High API latency causes orders to miss trading windows, resulting in stale price fills and degraded strategy performance not observed during backtesting

core.when: When initializing ensemble agent for multi-window inference
core.action: load and validate each agent models (A2C, PPO, DDPG, SAC, TD3) before ensemble execution

consequence.description: Missing agent model causes ensemble strategy to fail mid-execution, leaving incomplete backtest results across trading windows

core.when: When comparing multiple DRL strategy performances
core.action: Drop NaN rows before computing per-strategy statistics to avoid biased results

consequence.description: NaN values in merged strategy results cause Sharpe and return calculations to fail or produce misleading averages across strategies

core.when: When implementing performance evaluation metrics
core.action: Calculate daily return using simple first-difference percentage change of cumulative account value — do not use log returns which would complicate interpretation of absolute performance and create inconsistency with the framework's expected metric conventions

consequence.description: Using log returns changes the aggregation and interpretation of daily performance metrics, creating inconsistency with other framework components that expect simple returns and potentially causing misalignment in Sharpe ratio and other performance calculations

core.when: When implementing EI3 policy architecture for financial time series processing
core.action: Implement multi-scale convolutional filters capturing short-term momentum, medium-term trends, and long-term mean reversion simultaneously; verify filter kernel sizes match the intended temporal scales

consequence.description: Using single-scale convolutions or incorrect filter sizes fails to capture important temporal patterns, reducing strategy effectiveness across different market regimes and leading to poor backtest performance

core.when: When training portfolio optimization agents with epsilon-greedy exploration
core.action: Modify the epsilon exploration parameter from its empirically tuned value without re-evaluating convergence behavior and final policy performance

consequence.description: Epsilon values that are too high cause random exploration to dominate, preventing the agent from learning meaningful trading patterns; values too low cause premature convergence to suboptimal policies, both leading to significantly degraded backtest performance

core.when: When implementing GPM policy network and action selection in portfolio optimization
core.action: Change the softmax temperature parameter from its calibrated value without validating the resulting action distribution matches expected policy behavior

consequence.description: Temperature values that are too low produce near-deterministic policies that overfit to specific market conditions; values too high produce uniform random exploration that ignores learned patterns, both causing backtest-live inconsistency

core.when: When the EAD module is not yet fully implemented
core.action: obtain EAD values from external data sources for current operations

consequence.description: End-to-end ECL pipeline cannot execute, blocking regulatory reporting and provisioning processes. The placeholder status must be explicitly documented and flagged for full implementation.

core.when: When using external EAD data sources
core.action: validate that external EAD values align with internal loan schedules and limit data

consequence.description: Mismatch between external EAD values and internal systems can cause inconsistent provisioning, leading to regulatory non-compliance and potential audit failures.

core.when: When excluding observations from staging analysis, defaulted accounts at observation point must be excluded
core.action: exclude accounts that are already in default (BUCKETS >= 4) at the observation point from backward transaction analysis

consequence.description: Including defaulted accounts in backward transaction analysis introduces survivorship bias, as these accounts cannot transition through stages like non-defaulted accounts

core.when: When designing the model validation test suite, each four FWL assumption tests must be included in the validation pipeline
core.action: Include normality test, homoskedasticity test, autocorrelation test, and multicollinearity test in the validation scope

consequence.description: Omitting any FWL assumption test leaves undetected model misspecification that corrupts PD estimates and violates IFRS 9 regulatory requirements

core.when: When implementing PD calibration
core.action: Verify that random initial guess converges reliably — if calibration produces different results across runs, set deterministic seed or switch to weighted least squares for more stable convergence

consequence.description: Random initial guesses in non-convex optimization can converge to local minima, producing inconsistent calibration results across runs that corrupt PD estimates and violate regulatory reproducibility requirements

core.when: When implementing LGD beta distribution modeling
core.action: Test for multimodality in recovery rate distributions; if mixture patterns exist, evaluate logistic-normal or mixture distributions as alternatives to capture multimodality that single-mode beta distribution cannot represent

consequence.description: Single-mode beta distribution cannot capture multimodality in recovery outcomes caused by different collateral types or recovery processes; without testing for mixture patterns, ECL underestimates loss for multimodal segments

core.when: When setting random seed for reproducible simulation results
core.action: document the seed value used and its purpose (model validation, testing, or audit trail)

consequence.description: Without documented seed, simulation results cannot be replicated for model validation, regulatory audit, or debugging purposes, violating actuarial standards for model reproducibility.

core.when: When processing time series data for stationary bootstrap calibration
core.action: Enforce minimum 9 observations requirement before running bootstrap calibration — reject or flag datasets with fewer observations as insufficient for reliable spectral density estimation

consequence.description: Running bootstrap calibration with fewer than 9 observations produces unreliable spectral estimates with insufficient blocks for meaningful resampling, leading to invalid confidence intervals that misrepresent uncertainty in backtest results

core.when: When computing life contingencies with Actuarial object
core.action: Use float arithmetic for discount factors consistent with the library's design choice

consequence.description: The library consistently uses float division for discount factors (1 / (1 + i)). While Decimal would provide higher precision for financial calculations, the library's architecture assumes float. Mixing Decimal with float in the same calculation chain produces inconsistent results

core.when: When calculating security allocation amounts
core.action: Divide percent value by 100 before multiplying with portfolio total value

consequence.description: Allocation amounts will be 100x larger than intended, causing over-investment in securities and potential margin calls or regulatory violations

core.when: When implementing trade decision logic
core.action: apply deviation threshold check before price threshold checks

consequence.description: Current code has redundant if-else branches that produce identical outcomes regardless of deviation check, causing unnecessary API calls and potential order failures

core.when: When implementing or refactoring price collection and timestamp handling
core.action: Assume price data timestamps are unambiguous; the framework does not clearly distinguish between as-of date and processing timestamp

consequence.description: Without clear as-of vs processing time distinction, prices may be attributed to incorrect trading days, causing incorrect historical valuations and P&L calculations

core.when: When implementing or refactoring any stochastic components
core.action: Assume stochastic operations produce consistent results across runs; the framework does not verify random seed coverage for each random number generators

consequence.description: Without proper random seed management, backtest results become non-reproducible, making it impossible to validate strategy performance consistently

core.when: When implementing any stochastic operations or random sampling
core.action: Initialize random seed explicitly at system startup and propagate seed configuration to each random number generators; document each stochastic operations requiring seed management

consequence.description: Inconsistent random seeds across runs produce non-reproducible backtest results, preventing strategy validation and regulatory audit trails

core.when: When implementing position status updates in the accounting stage
core.action: Use each four position status values (INITIAL=1, IS_OPEN=2, IS_CLOSE=3, NOT_VERIFIED=4) correctly — IS_OPEN for active positions, IS_CLOSE for closing transactions, NOT_VERIFIED for positions pending external confirmation; do not assume positions are verified by default

consequence.description: Assuming positions are verified by default causes unconfirmed positions to be treated as settled, leading to incorrect portfolio valuation and potential settlement failures when external confirmations do not arrive

core.when: When implementing multi-provider data collection architecture
core.action: Implement provider priority configuration with explicit fallback routing and isolate each provider's credentials in separate secure storage (environment variables or secrets manager)

consequence.description: Missing provider priority routing causes backtest failures when the primary data source becomes unavailable, and credential leakage creates security risks in production deployments

core.when: When designing feature extraction pipelines for time-series data
core.action: Implement explicit time boundary validation — verify features at timestamp T use only data available up to T, validate no future data leaks via unit tests with synthetic future data

consequence.description: Feature extraction without time boundary validation introduces lookahead bias that inflates backtest returns by 10-50%, causing live trading performance to fall far below backtested results

core.when: When initializing banks from EBA CSV data
core.action: Split CSV rows using space delimiter as the data format specifies

consequence.description: The EBA_2018.csv uses space-separated values with no quoted fields; using comma splitting would misalign columns, causing bank_name, CET1E, leverage to be incorrectly parsed and balance sheet calculations to fail

core.when: When preventing zero-quantity or zero-price sales
core.action: Skip sale execution when asset price or computed quantity is effectively zero

consequence.description: Executing sales with zero price or zero quantity causes division errors or incorrect cash accounting, corrupting the market clearing settlement

core.when: When modeling market clearing behavior
core.action: Batch each asset sales before computing price impact when SIMULTANEOUS_FIRESALE=True

consequence.description: Sequential execution with price signals affecting subsequent sales creates look-ahead bias, preventing realistic modeling of illiquid market dynamics

core.when: When iterating over agents in each simulation timestep
core.action: shuffle agent list randomly before processing to verify order independence in the simultaneous firesale scenario

consequence.description: Systematic processing order will bias results as earlier agents get better prices in sequential clearing, creating non-reproducible and unfair outcomes

core.when: When implementing market clearing for fire sale simulations
core.action: Validate that the selected clearing mode (SIMULTANEOUS_FIRESALE or sequential) matches the intended scenario — if strategy depends on a specific mode, the alternative mode must produce comparable results for robustness

consequence.description: Using incorrect clearing mode produces materially different market outcomes, causing strategy backtests to be invalid for scenarios with different execution assumptions

core.when: When implementing the fire sale clearing logic during market stress events
core.action: Process each fire sale orders simultaneously before computing price impact — aggregate each sell orders first, then calculate price impact once on the combined quantity, not sequentially on each individual order

consequence.description: Sequential price impact application underestimates the true market impact of aggregate fire sales, causing the stress test to overstate remaining portfolio value during realistic liquidity crises

core.when: When implementing or extending initialization and scheduling logic in the trading framework
core.action: Assume trading calendar operations work correctly without explicit isolation from system calendar operations; trading calendar interactions with system calendar can cause incorrect scheduling and missed trading windows

consequence.description: Without explicit trading calendar isolation, batch jobs and scheduled tasks may execute on non-trading days or miss trading windows, causing strategies to fail or positions to remain unmanaged during critical periods

core.when: When implementing data initialization or lookback logic for historical data queries
core.action: Assume the framework provides point-in-time data availability — historical data queries may return current values rather than values as of a specific date; the framework does not implement temporal data versioning

consequence.description: Without point-in-time data handling, historical backtests use current values for past dates, introducing look-ahead bias that makes backtest results completely non-reproducible in live trading

core.when: When using Yahoo Finance historical data
core.action: Apply data cleaning to handle NaN values and weekend entries before using data

consequence.description: Yahoo Finance data may contain NaN values and weekend entries that cause incorrect calculation results in backtesting and analysis

core.when: When implementing fundinfo.price DataFrame structure
core.action: verify DataFrame contains date, netvalue, totvalue, and comment columns

consequence.description: Missing required columns causes downstream trade and backtest calculations to fail with KeyError or NaN values, corrupting financial analysis results

core.when: When parsing redemption fee information from Chinese text
core.action: handle feeinfo list as alternating [description, percentage] pairs with correct length validation

consequence.description: Incorrect fee parsing causes wrong redemption fee calculations, leading to inaccurate PnL reports and potential financial loss in backtest simulations

core.when: When initializing policy base class
core.action: Use record class inheritance and set self.status DataFrame with date and code columns

consequence.description: Without proper record inheritance and status DataFrame structure, downstream modules (trade, backtest) cannot consume the policy output, causing AttributeError or TypeError during backtesting

core.when: When initializing scheduled policy with times parameter
core.action: Pass times list as dates within the price data range to verify policy has valid trading dates

consequence.description: Scheduling trades on dates without price data causes KeyError or empty DataFrame issues in status_gen, preventing backtesting from running

core.when: When grid policy initialization
core.action: Assert buypercent and sellpercent lists have equal length to verify grid levels are symmetrically defined

consequence.description: Mismatched grid level lists cause index out of bounds errors during status_gen sell logic, crashing backtesting when accessing sellpts[i]

core.when: When processing price DataFrames across each modules
core.action: Use date column as primary key, formatted as pd.Timestamp or string YYYY-MM-DD

consequence.description: Inconsistent date formats across modules will cause join/merge failures and incorrect date-based filtering in trade and backtest operations

core.when: When processing real-time market data feeds for order execution
core.action: Assume market data timestamps represent as-of times — the framework does not distinguish between data timestamp and processing timestamp, treating each data as immediately available

consequence.description: Without as-of vs processing time distinction, backtests may use data that was not yet published at the claimed timestamp, introducing look-ahead bias that causes live trading underperformance

core.when: When processing fund data or calculating positions at historical dates
core.action: Assume the framework provides point-in-time data - the framework lacks historical snapshot capability and uses current data for historical calculations

consequence.description: Without point-in-time data, historical backtests use current fund information for past dates, causing look-ahead bias where fund mergers, splits, or fee changes are incorrectly applied retroactively

core.when: When implementing dividend data fetching
core.action: fall back to quote's original dividend data when stock-dividend service request fails

consequence.description: Backtest may receive future dividend data if service returns insufficient historical records, causing look-ahead bias in stock strategies

core.when: When orders match
core.action: Execute all-or-nothing fills with no partial fills

consequence.description: Backtest results show full fills but real exchanges may partially fill, causing live execution to fail or underfill orders

core.when: When checking order insert validity
core.action: Verify order is within trading time before accepting

consequence.description: Orders accepted outside trading hours execute at stale prices, causing incorrect fills and broken backtest results

core.when: When implementing financial ratio calculations that require cross-statement data alignment
core.action: Implement period intersection logic to find common dates across income statement, balance sheet, and cash flow before calculating ratios; discard periods where any required statement is missing data

consequence.description: Without period intersection, ratios may combine Q1 income statement with Q2 balance sheet, producing nonsensical financial metrics; backtesting strategies on misaligned ratios creates systematic errors that don't reflect actual company performance

core.when: When executing AI-generated code during SEC document processing evaluation
core.action: Enforce a hard 30-second execution timeout as a resource management guardrail; terminate any operation exceeding this limit

consequence.description: Operations exceeding the 30-second timeout are forcibly terminated, causing legitimate computationally intensive valid code to fail; backtest results may be incomplete if complex calculations cannot finish within the time limit

core.when: When calculating net change in holdings from ownership filings
core.action: Calculate net change in holdings as the arithmetic sum of transaction shares where disposed shares are recorded as negative values and acquired shares are recorded as positive values

consequence.description: Incorrect sign convention will invert position change calculations, causing net holdings to show disposals as acquisitions and vice versa, leading to fundamentally wrong portfolio positions in backtesting

core.when: When implementing period-over-period financial calculations using pandas shift(1) operations
core.action: Verify common periods are established across each financial statements (per BD-045) before applying shift(1) — verify chronological alignment of data spans to prevent comparing mismatched time periods (e.g., Q1 income with Q2 balance sheet)

consequence.description: shift(1) on misaligned data produces meaningless ratios by comparing income statement periods with balance sheet periods that do not correspond chronologically, silently corrupting ROA, leverage, and other key metrics

core.when: When setting context language for French or other non-UTF-8 encoded datasets
core.action: Verify that context.lang matches the actual dataset encoding (e.g., cp1252 for French data), and verify encoding is explicitly set rather than relying on UTF-8 defaults; validate that LLM name cleaning receives correctly decoded text

consequence.description: When context.lang assumes UTF-8 for cp1252-encoded French data, accented characters (é, è, ç) become garbled during LLM name cleaning, producing incorrect name normalizations that break entity matching for French entities in compliance workflows

core.when: When creating NPA status change records
core.action: create a Loan NPA Log entry with timestamp, classification reason, and event source

consequence.description: NPA history tracking is incomplete, making audit trail impossible and regulatory reporting inaccurate

core.when: When classifying loans without existing unpaid demands
core.action: set DPD to 0 and reset NPA status

consequence.description: Loans with no outstanding demands incorrectly retain NPA status, causing wrong provisioning and regulatory reporting

core.when: When implementing portfolio rebalancing logic
core.action: Assume the framework provides an automatic rebalancing trigger mechanism — the framework does not implement rebalancing triggers; rebalancing must be initiated externally or custom-coded

consequence.description: Without rebalancing triggers, loan portfolios may drift from target allocation, causing regulatory capital requirement violations and unexpected risk exposure

core.when: When implementing bucket cleanup retention
core.action: set default retention period to at least 30 days for data safety

consequence.description: Shorter retention periods risk permanent data loss before users can recover from accidental deletions or perform compliance audits

core.when: When implementing the log-first architecture for transaction processing
core.action: Produce immutable logs as the source of truth for every state change; derive each balance and volume state from log recomputation, never update in-place

consequence.description: State-first updates with in-place modifications break the audit trail and temporal queries; historical balances cannot be reconstructed, making compliance audits and error investigation impossible

core.when: When implementing or substituting the Store interface for balance queries in numscript execution
core.action: Verify that the Store implementation is deterministic and read-only during VM execution — verify any custom Store implementation maintains these properties or document deviations clearly

consequence.description: Non-deterministic or write-capable Store implementations cause inconsistent execution results; strategies that pass with StaticStore may fail with live storage, making backtest-to-production transitions unreliable

core.when: When implementing bucket cleanup with soft delete + 30-day delayed hard delete pattern
core.action: Verify that the 30-day recovery window matches actual business requirements — document whether deletions are expected to be mostly accidental before relying on this assumption

consequence.description: If deletions are intentional rather than accidental (e.g., user-initiated account closures), the 30-day window creates unnecessary storage overhead and potential GDPR/compliance issues with retaining data longer than needed

core.when: When deploying the framework or configuring data source API keys
core.action: Verify each required API keys are properly configured during deployment; do not rely on lazy initialization with graceful fallback to mask missing configuration in production environments

consequence.description: Relying on graceful fallback for missing API keys causes silent data source failures where functions return empty or null data, leading to incorrect backtest results without obvious error messages

core.when: When executing batch data fetching operations for large-scale historical data
core.action: Implement error handling with circuit-breaker pattern: track consecutive failures, halt batch processing after N failures (configurable threshold), require manual review before resuming; log each failed symbol with error reason for debugging

consequence.description: Sequential batch processing without circuit-breaker allows partial failures to cascade, resulting in incomplete datasets where missing symbols cause silent backtest errors or strategy decisions based on incomplete price history

core.when: When reading pricing and run summary results
core.action: pass correct locale indicator ('en' for Generic, 'cn' for SPV) to reader functions

consequence.description: Pricing and summary DataFrames will have wrong column headers, breaking financial metric calculations and regulatory reporting requirements

core.when: When structuring clean-out repurchase transaction ordering in waterfall
core.action: Execute sellAsset before transfer and payment actions in waterfall — enforce strict sequence: sellAsset → transfer → accrue → pay

consequence.description: Executing payments before asset sale settlement leaves repurchase proceeds unaccounted for and violates transaction atomicity, potentially causing cash to be distributed before asset title transfer completes

core.when: When implementing signal generation and order execution logic
core.action: Assume the framework handles bar execution timing — the framework does not implement bar-level synchronization for signal-to-order timing; signals may be processed without proper bar alignment

consequence.description: Without bar execution timing control, orders may execute on stale bars or with look-ahead bias, causing backtest results to diverge from live trading execution

core.when: When implementing portfolio optimization logic
core.action: Assume the framework provides complete optimization constraint handling — the framework may not validate constraint consistency, feasibility, or bound satisfaction for each optimization problems

consequence.description: Without constraint validation, optimization may produce infeasible portfolios that violate regulatory limits, causing execution failures or compliance violations

core.when: When initializing stochastic model components or running production simulations
core.action: Assume the framework provides complete random seed coverage for each stochastic processes; not each random number generators are seeded by default

consequence.description: Without complete random seed coverage, stochastic model components produce non-reproducible results across runs, making it impossible to reproduce model outputs for audit, validation, or regulatory review purposes

core.when: When implementing portfolio position management logic
core.action: Treat cash as a fungible scalar quantity supporting additive aggregation for netted calculations; implement equity positions as individually tracked position objects for dividend and tax lot accounting

consequence.description: Mixing cash and equity in the same container or applying additive aggregation to equity shares causes dividend calculations and tax lot tracking to produce incorrect results, leading to regulatory compliance violations and inaccurate P&L reporting

core.when: When implementing tax loss harvesting queries
core.action: Use TODAY() function in SQL queries instead of hardcoded dates

consequence.description: Hardcoded dates cause look-ahead bias where future information influences current recommendations, and queries become stale over time

core.when: When implementing hierarchical balance aggregation with recursive subtree computation
core.action: Validate account hierarchy for circular parent references before running balance aggregation; implement cycle detection or use iterative depth-first traversal with visited tracking

consequence.description: Circular parent references in the account hierarchy cause infinite recursion during subtree balance computation, leading to stack overflow crashes and complete backtesting failure with no partial results returned

core.when: When implementing or refactoring marginal tax rate calculation between tax brackets
core.action: Detect and handle zero Δ_proceeds between brackets that yields undefined infinite marginal rate — return a sentinel value or skip to next valid bracket range

consequence.description: Division by zero from zero Δ_proceeds crashes the tax calculation module, causing backtest pipeline failure and preventing lot-selection optimization

core.when: When the calendar.json file becomes outdated
core.action: claim calendar data is current without verifying against authoritative exchange sources

consequence.description: Outdated calendar files cause false non-trading day warnings for newly listed trading days, missing valid data and causing incorrect analysis

core.when: When integrating external calendar sources
core.action: validate calendar coverage includes historical dates before assuming complete date range

consequence.description: Calendar data sourced from Sina Finance may have gaps in historical coverage, causing false positives for valid trading days in historical analysis

core.when: When implementing or refactoring HTTP request handling in data fetching layer
core.action: Use a single unified HTTP client with consistent retry strategy across each data fetching modules - do not maintain parallel HTTP request systems with different retry behaviors

consequence.description: Inconsistent retry behavior across modules causes unpredictable API success rates, leading to incomplete data fetching and corrupted backtest results that don't reflect actual market data availability

core.when: When implementing or using numerical pricing models (finite difference, binomial tree, Monte Carlo)
core.action: Assume the framework implements model calibration residual tracking and convergence diagnostics — the framework lacks these production-critical diagnostics capabilities

consequence.description: Without convergence diagnostics, failed model calibrations produce silently incorrect option prices that pass all checks; deep ITM options priced with unconverged parameters show 5-15% valuation errors, creating systematic mispricing in live trading

core.when: When implementing finite difference option pricing methods
core.action: Assume the framework validates finite difference grid stability — the framework does not implement CFL condition checks or stability metrics; unstable grids execute without warning

consequence.description: Unstable finite difference grids produce exponentially growing numerical oscillations; without stability checks, option prices diverge to infinity or become NaN, causing portfolio valuation failures in live trading systems

core.when: When designing multi-agent trading architectures using this framework
core.action: Implement an external multi-agent orchestration layer with decision consensus and risk veto rights — define agent roles, voting thresholds, and risk veto conditions in configuration; verify each agent decisions pass through a central risk controller before order submission

consequence.description: Missing multi-agent consensus leads to conflicting orders from different agents; in live trading, this causes position overselling, margin violations, and potential regulatory breaches when automated systems make inconsistent decisions

core.when: When processing PCAF data quality assessments
core.action: Assume the framework provides PCAF data quality scoring — the framework does not implement PCAF-compliant data quality scoring; data quality issues will pass undetected into calculations

consequence.description: Without PCAF data quality scoring, corrupted or incomplete data sources are processed without flags, propagating errors into risk calculations and regulatory reports

core.when: When defining state space features for RL agents
core.action: Include features in the state space that contain future information relative to the decision timestamp — the framework does not prevent future data leakage into state features

consequence.description: Including future-leaked features in state space causes the RL agent to learn patterns dependent on information unavailable at decision time, making learned policies useless in live trading

core.when: When constructing state features from covariates in RL or ML pipelines
core.action: Include covariate values in state features before those covariates are temporally available — the framework does not enforce time-based availability constraints on covariates

consequence.description: Using temporally unavailable covariates in state space introduces look-ahead bias, causing RL policies to appear highly profitable in backtesting but lose money in live trading where those values don't exist yet

core.when: When implementing early stopping logic in training loop
core.action: Monitor validation MRR and halt training when MRR plateaus for 10 consecutive epochs — use MRR-based early stopping rather than fixed epoch counts to prevent overfitting

consequence.description: Using fixed epoch counts without early stopping risks overfitting when validation MRR plateaus, wasting computational resources and producing models that generalize poorly to live trading scenarios

core.when: When implementing graph construction or data loading logic in temporal knowledge graph modeling
core.action: Maintain three separate subgraph types: prior graph (historical context without targets), batch graph (target edges to predict), and cumulative graph (persistent entity state) — do not merge into a single graph

consequence.description: Merging context and target edges into a single graph causes data leakage where the model sees future information during training, making predictions systematically overconfident and backtest results non-reproducible

core.when: When implementing or modifying MRR computation logic in evaluation code
core.action: Use average rank for ties when computing MRR — do not implement random tie-breaking or single-rank assignment for duplicate prediction scores

consequence.description: Modifying tie-breaking logic introduces non-determinism into evaluation metrics, making backtest results non-reproducible and preventing fair model comparison across experiments

core.when: When configuring data provider connections in event-driven core
core.action: Assume the framework automatically handles provider priority and credential isolation — the framework does not implement provider priority or credential management; each providers share the same connection context

consequence.description: Without provider priority, backtests fail completely when a single data provider is unavailable; without credential isolation, API keys may be exposed across different provider configurations

core.when: When preparing datasets for machine learning model training and validation
core.action: Split data temporally into TRAIN, VALID, TEST segments using date range boundaries — verify each TRAIN timestamps precede VALID, which precedes TEST

consequence.description: Random or incorrect temporal splitting introduces look-ahead bias where future information leaks into training data, causing backtest results to significantly overestimate live trading performance

core.when: When configuring default price period for screeners
core.action: Use 2-year price period as default for 200-day MA calculations

consequence.description: Using a shorter price period than 2 years causes insufficient data for 200-day moving average and Minervini template checks. The default '2y' period provides approximately 504 trading days, enough for 200-day MA calculations plus Minervini uptrend verification.

core.when: When processing incomplete weekly data
core.action: Exclude incomplete current week from weekly pattern analysis

consequence.description: Including incomplete current week causes look-ahead bias, as future data would not be available in live trading

core.when: When implementing pattern detector execution framework
core.action: Use deterministic sequential execution order for the 7 specialized pattern detectors instead of parallel execution to verify reproducible signal generation and consistent ranking across runs

consequence.description: Parallel execution introduces non-determinism where identical scans produce different signal rankings due to race conditions in score aggregation, breaking backtesting reliability and user-facing alert stability guarantees

core.when: When implementing crossover detection patterns
core.action: Skip shift(1) with reasoning that 'the data looks clean' or 'we need the current bar signal immediately'

consequence.description: Rationalizing look-ahead bias introduction causes future information to leak into current signals, producing unrealistic backtest results that fail in live trading

core.when: When estimating VVIX when actual data is unavailable
core.action: use formula vvix = vix * 1.2 + 20 as fallback estimation

consequence.description: Using incorrect VVIX estimation formula will produce unreliable volatility stress scores, compromising margin call risk accuracy

core.when: When parsing Form 4 XML data
core.action: skip validation assuming well-formed XML since SEC EDGAR contains malformed filings and encoding variations

consequence.description: Parsing failures will silently drop transactions, creating survivorship bias where only clean filings contribute to sentiment

core.when: When executing walk-forward validation
core.action: Maintain strict temporal ordering: each training indices precede validation indices in each fold

consequence.description: Any shuffle or random sampling within TimeSeriesSplit introduces look-ahead bias, inflating validation metrics and producing misleading live trading expectations

core.when: When calculating the historical probability time series
core.action: Apply proper rolling window calculation using only data available up to each historical date point

consequence.description: Using future data in historical calculations introduces look-ahead bias, causing historical probabilities to appear more accurate than they actually were and invalidating backtested performance metrics

core.when: When implementing cross-validation logic for time series forecasting
core.action: Use expanding or rolling window splits that preserve temporal ordering; verify training data chronologically precedes validation data in every fold

consequence.description: Using random train/test splits or k-fold cross-validation on time series data introduces look-ahead bias, causing backtest results to appear significantly better than live performance

core.when: When processing stock universe for factor computation
core.action: load entire stock list into memory at once

consequence.description: 2H4G server will experience out-of-memory error, causing factor computation to fail and no recommendations available for that trading day

core.when: When running factor computation on non-trading days
core.action: skip computation and log informational message

consequence.description: Wasted API quota and computation resources on days with no market data, potentially depleting rate limit tokens before critical trading days

core.when: When scheduling triggers portfolio snapshots at 11PM
core.action: Portfolio snapshot must skip recording if current prices are unavailable for any held asset, and must NOT use average cost as fallback

consequence.description: Using average cost instead of current prices corrupts PnL calculations, producing inaccurate cumulative and daily PnL figures in portfolio history

core.when: When returning timestamps in any API response
core.action: Format each timestamps using ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS) in API responses

consequence.description: Non-ISO timestamp formats cause frontend parsing failures, inconsistent timezone handling, and broken date-based filtering in UI components

core.when: When converting exchange data to DataFrame
core.action: Convert OHLCV columns to float type before any indicator calculations

consequence.description: TA-LIB indicators assume float inputs and will raise exceptions when receiving integer timestamps or volumes, causing backtesting to crash

core.when: When designing a strategy with short or leverage trading
core.action: set INTERFACE_VERSION to 3

consequence.description: INTERFACE_VERSION=2 lacks short/leverage support. Strategies with can_short=True using INTERFACE_VERSION=2 may load but short signals will be ignored or produce undefined behavior. The framework explicitly documents INTERFACE_VERSION=3 as the first version supporting futures features (interface.py:66-67).

core.when: When managing Order to Trade relationships
core.action: Maintain full order audit trail with one-to-many Order-to-Trade relationship

consequence.description: Breaking order relationships causes broken audit trails where trade history cannot be reconstructed, preventing proper performance analysis and compliance reporting

core.when: When configuring detail timeframe for backtesting
core.action: Set detail timeframe equal to or larger than strategy timeframe

consequence.description: Backtest will fail with OperationalException when detail timeframe is not smaller than main strategy timeframe

core.when: When preparing historical data for backtesting
core.action: Verify startup candle count equals or exceeds longest indicator lookback period

consequence.description: Insufficient startup candles cause indicators to reference future data, introducing look-ahead bias that inflates backtest performance

core.when: When simulating entry signals in backtesting
core.action: Process entry signals at current candle open price, not future candles

consequence.description: Entry signals using future price data create look-ahead bias, causing backtest results to be unrealistically profitable

core.when: When simulating exit signals in backtesting
core.action: Execute exit signals at next candle open price, not current candle

consequence.description: Exit signals using current candle prices create look-ahead bias, causing trades to exit at unrealistically good prices

core.when: When calculating Sharpe ratio in backtesting
core.action: Return -100 for zero standard deviation to indicate non-optimal result

consequence.description: Zero standard deviation with positive Sharpe indicates insufficient data quality, must be flagged as non-optimal

core.when: When using intra-candle price simulation
core.action: Assume high occurs before low for stoploss calculations

consequence.description: Backtesting cannot know intra-candle price movement; assuming wrong order causes inaccurate stoploss simulation

core.when: When exporting hyperopt parameters to strategy file
core.action: Manually copy exported .json parameters into strategy class attributes

consequence.description: Hyperopt only exports parameter files when FTHYPT_FILEVERSION >= 2 and disableparamexport is not set; parameters are not automatically loaded for live trading

core.when: When implementing IHyperOptLoss with timeframe-dependent calculations
core.action: Set hyperoptloss.__class__.timeframe from config before use in trials

consequence.description: Loss function receives incorrect timeframe value, causing time-dependent calculations (e.g., SharpeRatioDaily) to produce invalid results

core.when: When backtest results indicate signal misalignment
core.action: use unshifted signals directly for trade execution without the mandatory shift(1) delay

consequence.description: Backtest equity curve will exhibit look-ahead bias, making live trading returns appear significantly worse than backtested results

core.when: When implementing or modifying FreqAI data pipeline components
core.action: Violate DataFrame column ordering contracts — the framework's positional tuple indices (BD-133) depend on fixed column positions; any column deviation silently corrupts OHLCV prices, signals, and profit calculations simultaneously with no runtime errors

consequence.description: Column order violations combined with hardcoded tuple indices cause all downstream calculations to reference wrong data fields — backtest equity curves become meaningless and live trading executes on corrupted price/signal data

core.when: When validating column positions before passing data to profit calculations
core.action: Implement explicit column position validation at the entry point of profit calculation logic, verifying that price columns (open, high, low, close) are in expected positions before processing

consequence.description: Without column position validation, corrupted profit figures silently propagate through CAGR, Sharpe, Sortino, MaxDrawdown, and other metrics, causing strategy selection based on backtest results that bear no relation to actual expected performance

core.when: When resolving strategy configuration attributes during bot initialization
core.action: Follow the three-tier attribute override precedence: config values override strategy attributes, which override hardcoded defaults — debug configuration issues by tracing this cascade

consequence.description: Ignoring the attribute precedence precedence causes configuration values to be silently overridden, leading to strategies using unexpected parameter values and producing backtest results that differ from live trading

core.when: When implementing a CSV data feed
core.action: override _loadline method to parse tokens into OHLCV values

consequence.description: CSV data feed will fail to parse any market data because _loadline is the only hook for subclasses to implement token-to-value conversion, causing the entire backtest to receive no price data

core.when: When parsing datetime strings from CSV
core.action: use the exact dtformat parameter matching the source file format

consequence.description: Datetime parsing will raise ValueError at runtime when the format string doesn't match the actual date format in the CSV file, causing data loading to fail silently or crash the entire backtest

core.when: When converting OHLCV values from strings
core.action: wrap numeric conversion in try-except to handle empty or malformed fields

consequence.description: Malformed numeric values in CSV will raise ValueError during float conversion, aborting the entire backtest with unhandled exception and producing no results

core.when: When using CSV data feeds
core.action: pass preload=True or call data.preload() before running cerebro

consequence.description: Without preloading, live data feeds will cause cerebro to wait indefinitely for streaming data that doesn't exist in CSV files, causing the backtest to hang or fail immediately

core.when: When enabling replay mode on a data feed
core.action: enable preload=True because replay is incompatible with preloading

consequence.description: Preloading with replay produces incorrect backtest results because bars are pre-aggregated instead of built step-by-step

core.when: When handling late-arriving data in subday timeframes
core.action: configure takelate parameter appropriately (default True) to control late data acceptance

consequence.description: Late-arriving data mishandling causes bars to be incorrectly attributed to wrong time periods, corrupting backtest results

core.when: When implementing indicators that depend on future data or require non-causal look-ahead
core.action: access positive array indices that represent future bars relative to current processing position

consequence.description: Indicator will exhibit look-ahead bias, producing unrealistic signals that cannot be executed in live trading based on historical data

core.when: When accessing data in strategy next() methods
core.action: Use self.data0 or self.datas[0] for primary data, not hardcoded indices

consequence.description: Using wrong data index causes indicator calculations on incorrect data, leading to wrong trading signals and backtest failures

core.when: When tracking trades manually without using Trade API
core.action: Duplicate trade tracking logic already implemented by backtrader

consequence.description: Redundant trade tracking causes double-counting of positions and incorrect PnL calculations in trade history and analyzers

core.when: When implementing strategy that relies on order execution timing
core.action: Assume orders execute immediately in the same bar as submission

consequence.description: Market orders execute at next bar's open price in backtesting; assuming immediate execution causes look-ahead bias in backtest results

core.when: When implementing multiple strategy instances on same data
core.action: Use tradeid parameter to distinguish trades from different strategies

consequence.description: Without tradeid, trades from different strategies on same data get grouped together, causing incorrect position tracking and PnL attribution

core.when: When implementing Sharpe ratio calculations
core.action: Use risk-free rate conversion factor from RATEFACTORS table for timeframe matching

consequence.description: Sharpe ratio produces incorrect annualized values when risk-free rate is not converted to match the analysis timeframe, leading to misleading performance comparisons

core.when: When using the fundmode parameter in analyzers
core.action: Pass fund parameter to child analyzers for consistent NAV vs fund value selection

consequence.description: Inconsistent fundmode selection across child analyzers produces mismatched returns, positions, and leverage calculations in PyFolio output

core.when: When implementing strategies that use data filters or signals
core.action: rely on runonce=True vectorized execution mode

consequence.description: Data filters modify bars during iteration, which is incompatible with vectorized _runonce mode that loads all data upfront. Backtest results will be incorrect because filter transformations are not applied.

core.when: When cerebro.py:live=True is set for live trading mode
core.action: accept that both preload and runonce are automatically forced to False

consequence.description: Live mode disables vectorized execution to support tick-by-tick data delivery from live feeds. This reduces backtest speed but enables real-time data handling

core.when: When implementing data ingestion to data filtering data flow
core.action: Maintain chronological order with no temporal gaps in OHLCV bar timestamps

consequence.description: Backtest equity curve exhibits look-ahead bias when non-chronological data causes indicator values to reference future prices, leading to live trading returns that fall far below backtested results

core.when: When implementing data ingestion with exactbars=True
core.action: Set qbuffer memory saving mode when exactbars > 0 to prevent buffer overflow

consequence.description: Backtest consumes excessive memory when exactbars is enabled without corresponding qbuffer memory management

core.when: When implementing broker subclasses or using broker operations
core.action: Implement each abstract methods: getcash(), getvalue(), getposition(), submit(), cancel(), buy(), sell()

consequence.description: Missing broker method implementation causes runtime NotImplementedError exceptions, preventing order execution and backtest completion

core.when: When using Yahoo Finance data feeds or any web API data source
core.action: Account for data delivery delays — Yahoo API has known latency and historical data may have gaps

consequence.description: Backtest results are corrupted by missing or delayed data, causing incorrect signal timing and unreliable performance metrics

core.when: When implementing data filtering using resampler or replayer
core.action: Use filters to wrap feeds, returning False to pass bars through unchanged. This composition pattern preserves separation of concerns and allows chaining multiple transformations (resampling, session filling, data cleaning) without modifying the underlying feed.

consequence.description: Modifying resampler or replayer to behave as feeds breaks the filter composition pattern, causing data to be transformed incorrectly or skipped entirely during backtesting runs

core.when: When implementing or refactoring live data feed handling code
core.action: Call islive() method to determine data feed mode; when islive() returns True, disable preloading and runonce optimization in Cerebro to enable real-time bar delivery

consequence.description: Without calling islive(), preloading remains enabled in live trading mode causing artificial latency in bar delivery; orders may reference stale data or miss real-time execution opportunities

core.when: When configuring cheat-on-open execution in backtesting
core.action: Set both cerebro.p.cheat_on_open=True AND broker.p.coo=True explicitly; do not rely on cerebro default alone as the broker ignores cerebro's setting unless broker.coo is explicitly set

consequence.description: Relying only on cerebro.p.cheat_on_open=True without setting broker.p.coo=True causes next-bar execution instead of open-bar execution, making backtest results inconsistent with expected cheat-on-open behavior

core.when: When using preload=True with complex multi-timeframe strategies
core.action: Verify vectorization is active by checking that exactbars=False and replay mode is disabled; expect slower bar-by-bar execution if either is true

consequence.description: When exactbars mode or replay is enabled, vectorization is silently disabled causing 10-100x performance degradation without warning, making complex multi-timeframe backtests take unexpectedly long

core.when: When optimizing backtest execution speed with runonce=True
core.action: Verify each three vectorization dependencies are active: runonce=True, preload=True, and no indicators with only once() implementations; any break in the chain causes silent fallback to bar-by-bar execution

consequence.description: Breaking any link in the runonce-preload-once chain silently disables vectorization, causing 10-100x slower backtests without any warning or user notification, making performance optimization difficult to diagnose

core.when: When implementing data feed synchronization for non-master datas
core.action: Implement the peek-advance-check cycle: peek checks if next bar timestamp exceeds master timeline, advance adds current bar, check validates alignment — repeat until synchronized

consequence.description: Skipping the synchronization cycle causes timestamp gaps between master and non-master datas, corrupting backtesting accuracy and producing non-reproducible strategy results

core.when: When running backtests with large datasets or in memory-constrained environments
core.action: Set preload=False to enable streaming mode instead of preloading each data into memory — default preload=True may cause out-of-memory errors on large datasets

consequence.description: Default preload=True loads all historical data into memory before backtesting begins; for datasets spanning many years or many symbols, this causes out-of-memory errors that halt backtesting entirely, especially on systems with limited RAM

core.when: When implementing Double Exponential Moving Average (DEMA) indicator
core.action: Calculate DEMA using the formula: DEMA = 2*EMA(period) - EMA(EMA(period), period). This specific coefficient structure achieves approximately half the lag of standard EMA. Do not simplify or re-derive the formula.

consequence.description: Incorrect DEMA formula variations produce different lag characteristics that alter signal timing. Short-term strategies relying on DEMA crossovers will generate signals at different prices, causing backtest results to diverge from expected short-term trading behavior.

core.when: When using Ref operator with N=0
core.action: Use Ref($feature, 0) expecting accurate historical lookback calculation

consequence.description: Ref with N=0 logs warning that it will not be accurately calculated, leading to incorrect first-value semantics and potential look-ahead bias

core.when: When implementing PIT (Point-in-Time) database operations
core.action: Access future period data in PIT expressions due to data integrity constraints

consequence.description: PIT database is designed for point-in-time analysis and explicitly rejects expressions with right-extended window (future data access), raising ValueError to prevent look-ahead bias in historical backtesting

core.when: When implementing time-series shift operations
core.action: Use series.shift(N) for historical data access with N > 0, and handle N < 0 for future data access

consequence.description: Incorrect shift semantics causes wrong temporal alignment of data, producing features that reference incorrect time periods and corrupting backtest results

core.when: When using TSDataSampler with large datasets
core.action: be aware that the sampler converts DataFrame to numpy arrays which may change data types, affecting precision for monetary calculations

consequence.description: Data type conversion from DataFrame to numpy array causes loss of decimal precision, potentially affecting financial calculations in the strategy

core.when: When implementing strategy-to-exchange order flow
core.action: route each orders through Exchange.deal_order for proper cost modeling and position tracking

consequence.description: Direct position manipulation bypasses cost calculations and limit checks, producing inaccurate PnL and violating market microstructure modeling

core.when: When initializing policy in SAOEIntStrategy
core.action: set policy to eval() mode after initialization by checking self._policy.eval()

consequence.description: Policy remains in training mode causing incorrect action selection during backtest due to dropout/batchnorm differences

core.when: When using ROLL_SD (sliding window) for rolling evaluation
core.action: Set specified step size to avoid data gaps between consecutive windows

consequence.description: Gaps in sliding window coverage leave temporal periods unevaluated, creating incomplete backtest results

core.when: When implementing or using any data expression in Qlib
core.action: Verify each timestamps in data use calendar index (integer index into trading calendar), not wall-clock datetime values

consequence.description: Data queries will return incorrect time ranges, backtest signals will be misaligned with actual trading calendar, and feature calculations will reference wrong time periods

core.when: When collecting data from Yahoo Finance
core.action: Validate and clean data before use — Yahoo Finance data is explicitly documented as 'might not be perfect'

consequence.description: Backtest results will be based on inaccurate price/volume data, causing incorrect strategy evaluation and potential financial loss

core.when: When implementing portfolio rebalancing logic
core.action: Execute rebalancing on a daily frequency to match feature generation and model prediction cycles

consequence.description: Using weekly or monthly rebalancing introduces look-ahead bias since the framework generates features daily; changes in market conditions between rebalancing dates would be invisible to the model

core.when: When implementing hierarchical backtesting with portfolio-level and stock-level decisions
core.action: Use nested decision execution structure to properly handle hierarchical strategy evaluation where high-level allocation interacts with low-level execution

consequence.description: Flat execution cannot properly represent hierarchical strategies where portfolio allocation decisions interact with stock-level execution, leading to incorrect backtest results for composite strategies

core.when: When implementing rolling calculations using Ref(expression, offset)
core.action: Use explicit finite offsets for rolling calculations (e.g., Ref(expr, -252) for 1-year window) - do not use Ref(expr, 0) as it returns infinite back-rolling causing unbounded memory consumption

consequence.description: Using Ref(expr, 0) causes unbounded memory growth on large datasets, eventually crashing the backtesting process or causing severe performance degradation as memory exhaustion approaches

core.when: When writing code that consumes feature storage lookup results
core.action: Use 'if result.empty' or 'if len(result) == 0' instead of 'if result is None' to check for missing data, since feature storage returns empty pd.Series with dtype=float32 rather than None

consequence.description: Code checking 'if result is None' will silently fail when data is missing, causing NaN values to propagate through backtest calculations and produce incorrect strategy performance metrics

core.when: When implementing data processing pipelines in Qlib backtesting
core.action: Follow the strict 3-stage contract: raw data (DK_R) must pass through inference stage (DK_I) for feature engineering before reaching learn stage (DK_L) — verify each stage receives properly transformed inputs

consequence.description: Bypassing the infer stage (DK_I) causes data leakage where raw features enter model training without proper transformation, systematically overstating backtest returns and causing live trading performance degradation

core.when: When writing daily bar data to bcolz format
core.action: verify each asset has exactly one row per trading session with no gaps or duplicates

consequence.description: The BcolzDailyBarWriter raises an AssertionError if the number of rows per asset does not match the expected session count. Missing or extra sessions cause incorrect data alignment during backtesting, leading to wrong price lookups.

core.when: When writing adjustment data to SQLite
core.action: verify dataframe columns match expected dtypes for splits, dividends, and mergers

consequence.description: SQLiteAdjustmentWriter validates column names and dtypes. Mismatched columns cause ValueError or TypeError, preventing correct corporate action adjustments and leading to incorrect historical prices.

core.when: When implementing a custom ingest function
core.action: write data through the provided writer objects rather than directly to files

consequence.description: Direct file manipulation bypasses the writer's validation and indexing logic, resulting in unreadable data or corrupted bcolz tables that crash the backtest engine.

core.when: When converting CSV dates to trading sessions
core.action: verify CSV dates are parseable as datetime and sorted in ascending order

consequence.description: CSV files with unsorted dates or unparseable date formats cause incorrect sid assignment and session alignment, resulting in corrupted historical price data for backtesting.

core.when: When implementing symbol ownership mapping for equity assets
core.action: create overlapping ownership periods for the same symbol in the same country

consequence.description: Overlapping symbol ownership periods cause ambiguous asset resolution, leading to incorrect security selection during backtesting and potentially trading the wrong asset

core.when: When creating continuous future contracts
core.action: specify adjustment parameter from allowed set {add, mul, None}

consequence.description: Invalid adjustment style causes ValueError at continuous future creation, breaking futures-based backtests

core.when: When computing asset lifetimes for a date range
core.action: handle NaN start_date as epoch (0) and NaN end_date as INT_MAX

consequence.description: Improper NaN handling causes assets with missing dates to be incorrectly filtered, resulting in missing price data during backtesting

core.when: When retrieving assets by SID during backtesting
core.action: reuse cached Asset objects from _asset_cache for repeated lookups

consequence.description: Without caching, millions of SQL queries occur during backtest, causing unacceptable slowdown

core.when: When validating asset data before database write
core.action: check for duplicate SID assignments in equities DataFrame

consequence.description: Duplicate SIDs cause database integrity errors and incorrect asset routing during backtesting

core.when: When retrieving ordered futures contracts
core.action: reuse _ordered_contracts cache to avoid repeated database queries

consequence.description: Without cache reuse, each get_ordered_contracts call executes SQL queries, causing severe performance degradation in futures-heavy backtests

core.when: When creating transactions from fill events
core.action: validate transaction magnitude is at least 1 share before creating a transaction

consequence.description: Zero-magnitude transactions would corrupt the portfolio ledger and produce incorrect PnL calculations with no economic substance

core.when: When applying slippage to calculate fill prices
core.action: verify buy orders fill at higher prices (positive impact) and sell orders fill at lower prices (negative impact) relative to the base price

consequence.description: Inverting the slippage direction would make buys cheaper and sells more expensive than in reality, producing unrealistic profits that would not be achievable in live trading

core.when: When marking portfolio dirty in setter
core.action: also mark account dirty when marking portfolio dirty to verify consistency

consequence.description: Account values may be calculated from stale portfolio state, causing inconsistent leverage and buying power calculations

core.when: When adjusting cost basis for commissions
core.action: increase cost basis for long positions and decrease for short positions

consequence.description: Commission-adjusted cost basis will not reflect true break-even prices, causing incorrect P&L calculations

core.when: When loading currency-converted equity pricing data
core.action: use shift=1 to align FX rates with prior-day prices

consequence.description: Incorrect shift causes look-ahead bias where future FX rates are used, producing misleading portfolio valuations that don't reflect actual trading conditions

core.when: When performing forward-fill lookup for FX rates
core.action: use searchsorted with side='right' and subtract 1 to get previous valid date

consequence.description: Incorrect searchsorted side causes wrong date alignment, using future FX rates that introduce look-ahead bias into portfolio valuations

core.when: When looking up assets by symbol strings
core.action: Use symbol strings only as lookup keys; each internal asset operations must use the integer SID for consistent referencing

consequence.description: Symbol ambiguity may cause wrong asset to be selected (multiple assets can share symbols over time), leading to trading wrong securities and corrupting backtest results

core.when: When implementing order direction logic in backtesting
core.action: Use positive order amounts for buy (long) positions and negative amounts for sell (short) positions — maintain consistent sign convention throughout order processing and position calculation

consequence.description: Inverting the order sign convention causes buy orders to be interpreted as sells and vice versa, resulting in completely opposite trading behavior with catastrophic losses in backtest and live trading

core.when: When ingesting custom CSV data bundles via directory-based CSV ingestion
core.action: Verify directory permissions are granted and CSV files conform to expected schemas before ingestion; inconsistent schemas or permission errors will cause silent data loading failures

consequence.description: CSV directory ingestion assumes unrestricted directory access and consistent file schemas; restrictive permissions or malformed CSVs cause silent loading failures that leave backtests with missing or partial data

core.when: When modifying performance-critical code paths
core.action: Use Cython for performance-critical components

consequence.description: The project uses Cython for performance-critical components. Using pure Python in these paths will significantly degrade backtesting performance

core.when: When processing adjustment for weekly bars
core.action: apply adjustment before resampling weekly bars to maintain correct temporal alignment

consequence.description: Incorrect bar resampling with adjustment causes OHLCV values to be misaligned with actual trading days

core.when: When using NEXT_BAR_OPEN matching type with 1d frequency
core.action: expect automatic degradation to CURRENT_BAR_CLOSE

consequence.description: NEXT_BAR_OPEN signals execute immediately at current bar close instead of next bar open, causing look-ahead bias and incorrect strategy behavior

core.when: When selecting matching type for minute-frequency backtests
core.action: use only NEXT_BAR_OPEN, VWAP, or CURRENT_BAR_CLOSE

consequence.description: Invalid matching type causes RuntimeError, preventing backtest from starting

core.when: When modifying or extending strategy execution behavior
core.action: Skip phase context enforcement even when code changes appear minor

consequence.description: Bypassing ExecutionContext phase enforcement enables API calls during invalid phases, causing look-ahead bias, incorrect data access, and potentially large financial losses from trades based on future information.

core.when: When implementing order submission logic in backtesting
core.action: Verify frontend validators execute before orders reach the broker — the validation boundary is at order submission time, before any matching logic executes

consequence.description: Bypassing frontend validation allows invalid orders to reach the matching engine, corrupting backtest simulation integrity and producing non-reproducible results that do not match live trading behavior

core.when: When fast-forwarding account state through historical data
core.action: Process OPEN position trades before CLOSE position trades to verify deterministic state transitions matching exchange semantics

consequence.description: Reversing the OPEN-before-CLOSE order causes non-deterministic position state when opening and closing the same instrument on the same bar, making backtest results unreproducible across runs

core.when: When scheduling minute-frequency events for non-Chinese stock markets
core.action: Use the framework's default scheduler minute offsets (571, 690, 780, 900) for Chinese A-share markets; these hardcoded values represent Chinese stock trading minutes from open and are incorrect for other markets

consequence.description: Using Chinese market minute offsets for non-Chinese stocks causes scheduled events to fire at wrong times relative to actual market hours, creating timing errors in strategy execution and backtest results that are difficult to diagnose

core.when: When implementing or modifying account margin calculation logic in portfolio accounting
core.action: Verify margin calculation is bypassed (returns 0) for stock-only accounts that never incur margin requirements; do not add conditional overhead to every margin access

consequence.description: Adding conditional checks to margin calculation for every access introduces unnecessary overhead for equity-only accounts, degrading backtesting performance significantly in long-running strategies

core.when: When implementing market order execution logic in backtesting
core.action: Cancel the unfilled portion of a market order when volume limit is exceeded — do NOT convert to limit order or keep it active

consequence.description: Keeping unfilled market order portions active introduces stale orders that may execute at unexpected future prices, causing the backtest to violate market order semantics and produce non-reproducible results

core.when: When integrating custom functions or external libraries into strategy execution environment
