core.action: Verify that strategy code executes within scope-based sandbox with explicit API whitelist — do not grant access to internal framework APIs or global namespace

consequence.description: Without proper sandboxing, buggy or malicious strategy code can corrupt system state or access internal APIs, leading to unpredictable backtest results and potential security vulnerabilities

core.when: When processing stock split adjustments in position management
core.action: Use ROUND_HALF_UP rounding mode for stock split quantity adjustments to verify position quantities remain integers after split processing

consequence.description: Using ROUND_HALF_EVEN or other rounding modes for stock splits may produce non-integer share counts, causing position quantity mismatches with broker records and incorrect P&L calculations in live trading

core.when: When debugging or logging in strategies
core.action: Use logger.info() for logging instead of print() statements

consequence.description: print() statements do not work properly in backtest environments and output is lost

core.when: When handling stock codes in data collection
core.action: verify stock code is 6-character string (zero-padded if needed) for consistent MongoDB matching

consequence.description: Stock code mismatch causes duplicate data entries or missing data during queries, corrupting backtest results

core.when: When incrementing stock day data in MongoDB
core.action: query the last stored date from database and only fetch data after that date

consequence.description: Re-downloading existing data causes duplicate entries and incorrect backtest results due to overlapping timestamps

core.when: When TDX API call fails due to network or server issues
core.action: retry up to 3 times with random delay between 50-100ms before failing

consequence.description: Single failed API call without retry causes data gap, leading to incomplete historical data for backtesting

core.when: When initializing a CTA strategy base class
core.action: auto-detect market type by checking whether the instrument code contains alphabetic characters

consequence.description: Incorrect market type classification causes wrong position tracking logic (futures vs stock T+1 handling), leading to trading rule violations or incorrect PnL calculations

core.when: When implementing market type detection for commission calculation
core.action: Detect market type from code format: alphanumeric codes are futures, numeric codes are stocks

consequence.description: Wrong market type detection causes commission to be calculated with incorrect formula, leading to significant cost miscalculation especially for futures

core.when: When calculating position cost basis and profit, the FIFO (First In First Out) matching rule MUST be used
core.action: Apply FIFO matching for each position calculations to verify consistent cost basis across the system

consequence.description: Portfolio PnL calculations will be inconsistent with regulatory reporting if non-FIFO matching is used, causing compliance issues in live trading

core.when: When modeling market types for different asset classes
core.action: Use the correct integer encoding: STOCK=0, FUTURE=2, CRYPTO=5 for market type classification

consequence.description: Wrong market type encoding causes incorrect margin calculations and trading rules, leading to margin calls or failed trades

core.when: When modeling financial calculations involving monetary values
core.action: Use specified precision for monetary calculations — avoid pure float for high-precision financial operations

consequence.description: Float precision errors accumulate over many trades, causing tiny but compounding discrepancies in account balances and PnL calculations

core.when: When implementing factor-based strategies using the factor base class
core.action: Always use the register method when creating new factors and follow the insert lifecycle for persistence; verify factors are properly registered to maintain registry integrity for lazy loading and factor lineage tracking

consequence.description: Unregistered factors break lazy loading functionality and lose factor lineage tracking; backtests become non-reproducible without proper factor registry, and compliance audit trails will have gaps making strategy validation impossible

core.when: When configuring the job scheduler for high-volume backtesting or live trading operations
core.action: Submit more than 100 concurrent jobs or 10000 pending jobs to the async scheduler — the framework enforces hard limits at these thresholds and will reject or fail excess jobs

consequence.description: Exceeding scheduler limits causes jobs to be rejected or queued indefinitely, resulting in missed trade executions or incomplete backtests that silently drop tasks without warning

core.when: When processing any stochastic operations in backtesting
core.action: Assume the framework provides comprehensive random seed handling for full reproducibility — random seed configuration is not fully covered across each modules

consequence.description: Without full random seed coverage, backtest results may vary between runs due to non-deterministic behavior in factor computation, making results non-reproducible and strategy comparisons unreliable

core.when: When configuring thread-based backtesting tasks via QA_Thread constructor
core.action: Change the daemon parameter from its default value (daemon=False) to daemon=True for long-running or critical pipeline operations

consequence.description: Setting daemon=True allows threads to be abruptly terminated when the main program exits, causing incomplete batch calculations, partial data writes, and silently corrupted backtest results that appear valid but are mathematically incomplete

core.when: When implementing signal functions that access historical bars
core.action: Use di parameter with value >= 1 to access historical bars via c.bars_raw[-di]

consequence.description: Using di < 1 or accessing future data via shift(-n) introduces look-ahead bias, causing backtest results to be unrealistically optimistic and live trading to fail

core.when: When computing technical analysis signals
core.action: Use shift(-n) to access future data in signal computation paths

consequence.description: shift(-n) in signal computation introduces look-ahead bias, causing backtest equity curve to show unrealistic returns that will not match live trading

core.when: When updating Position state with new signals
core.action: validate that signal datetime is strictly greater than previous signal datetime

consequence.description: Regressing timestamps corrupt position state history, causing incorrect trade pairing and distorted backtest performance metrics

core.when: When calculating max drawdown in performance analysis
core.action: Use cumulative maximum minus current value pattern to identify peak-to-trough decline

consequence.description: Max drawdown calculation will fail to identify the true peak-to-trough decline, leading to incorrect risk assessment and Calmar ratio

core.when: When using n1b (next-bar return) in edge calculations
core.action: Use shift(-1) to compute next-bar return from current-bar price

consequence.description: Edge calculation will use current-bar return instead of next-bar return, violating the one-bar delay principle and introducing look-ahead bias

core.when: When generating output reports from backtest analysis
core.action: Support both HTML and PDF formats as specified in output requirements

consequence.description: Report generation will fail for unsupported file formats, preventing users from obtaining backtest analysis results

core.when: When updating Position state with new signals
core.action: Verify new signal dt must be strictly greater than the previous signal dt — out-of-order signal injection causes state corruption

consequence.description: Out-of-order dt values will corrupt Position state, causing incorrect trade pair matching, wrong PnL calculations, and invalid backtest results where opening and closing trades appear in wrong chronological order

core.when: When representing position state across the system
core.action: Encode position state using ternary values: pos ∈ {-1, 0, 1} representing short, empty, long — consistent throughout system

consequence.description: Position values outside {-1, 0, 1} will cause incorrect trading logic in Position.update, wrong ensemble position calculations in CzscTrader.get_ensemble_pos, and invalid backtest statistics

core.when: When using CZSC environment variable configuration
core.action: Set CZSC_USE_PYTHON=true to force Python implementation when rs-czsc is installed — default behavior prefers Rust implementation

consequence.description: Without CZSC_USE_PYTHON set, the Rust implementation may lack certain features like dill serialization support, causing trader serialization to fail in CTA backtest results

core.when: When implementing or refactoring Position.update method
core.action: Retain the datetime (dt) sequence validation that raises an error when dt regresses; do not replace with silent ignore or log-and-continue patterns

consequence.description: Without dt regression validation, stale bars silently overwrite fresher data, causing P&L calculations to use out-of-sequence information and producing incorrect backtest results that cannot be reproduced in live trading

core.when: When implementing position management logic in backtesting or live trading
core.action: Use exactly 1 for long positions, 0 for neutral, and -1 for short positions — the Position class requires discrete integer values for correct position state determination throughout the risk control and trading execution pipeline

consequence.description: Using non-discrete values (e.g., floats, enums, or other encodings) causes position state determination to fail silently, leading to incorrect risk calculations and potentially catastrophic position mismatches in live trading

core.when: When implementing strategy position caching
core.action: Use MD5 hash of position config for cache key validation — any configuration change must trigger automatic cache invalidation to prevent stale position data from being reused across strategy versions

consequence.description: Without MD5-based cache invalidation, stale position data from previous strategy versions may be incorrectly reused, causing strategies to operate with outdated state and producing invalid backtest results

core.when: When initializing technical indicator calculations for trading signals
core.action: Require at least 600 bars of historical data before executing signal analysis, ensuring sufficient sample size for long-period indicators like MA250

consequence.description: Processing signals with insufficient bars causes indicators like moving averages to produce unreliable values, generating noisy and false trading signals that lead to unprofitable trades in live trading

core.when: When implementing or extending strategy classes that inherit from the base strategy abstraction
core.action: Implement the positions property as required by @abstractmethod decorator — subclasses MUST provide a valid positions attribute returning current position data

consequence.description: Missing positions implementation causes AttributeError at runtime when backtest accesses position data; the error only surfaces during backtest execution, not during development or import

core.when: When discretizing continuous factors into signal buckets
core.action: Use rolling quantile binning with historical data to determine bucket boundaries — do not use fixed quantile boundaries or equal-width bins

consequence.description: Using fixed quantile boundaries introduces look-ahead bias by using future data to determine bucket assignments, causing signal generation to appear more predictive than it actually is

core.when: When configuring the runtime environment
core.action: Use Rust-based rs-czsc as the primary implementation, falling back to Python only when Rust is unavailable or CZSC_USE_PYTHON is set

consequence.description: Running Python implementation when Rust is available results in 10-100x slower execution for core Chan Theory analysis operations, making large-scale backtesting impractical

core.when: When downloading multiple symbols into Data object
core.action: Verify each symbols share the same temporal index after alignment

consequence.description: VectorBT performs vectorized matrix operations across symbols. Misaligned indexes cause incorrect broadcast calculations, producing wrong trading signals and invalid backtest results. Matrix operations require uniform index across all symbols.

core.when: When downloading data from Yahoo Finance source
core.action: Use YFData as a reliable production data source

consequence.description: Yahoo Finance data is unstable: it may be manipulated, contain noise, or return missing data points (e.g., zero volume). Using this for live trading produces incorrect signals and financial losses. The warning explicitly states it is for demonstration only.

core.when: When implementing custom indicator functions with rolling windows
core.action: Set minp parameter to match the window size to verify minimum data points before output begins

consequence.description: Incorrect minp setting causes premature non-NaN values in rolling calculations, breaking the standard 'window size data points before valid output' domain invariant and corrupting early-indicator backtests

core.when: When using backward-fill operations (bfill) on time series
core.action: Use bfill functions in production indicator calculations

consequence.description: bfill operations look ahead to future values, introducing look-ahead bias that causes live trading results to differ significantly from backtests

core.when: When implementing look-ahead bias prevention in trading signals
core.action: Shift signals forward by one tick when using close price for signal generation

consequence.description: Signals generated on close price without shifting expose the strategy to look-ahead bias, causing backtest returns to overstate actual trading performance

core.when: When setting the wait parameter in future look-ahead indicators
core.action: Set wait >= 1 to exclude current value from future value calculations

consequence.description: wait=0 includes current bar data in future calculations, creating look-ahead bias that corrupts signal validity in live trading scenarios

core.when: When implementing indicators with random components
core.action: Set seed parameter to verify reproducible results

consequence.description: Without seed, random components produce different outputs each run, preventing result reproducibility and making backtest validation impossible

core.when: When using bshift for signal processing
core.action: recognize that bshift(-n) = pd.shift(n) and this operation looks ahead into future data

consequence.description: bshift is an explicit look-ahead operation. Using it without understanding the temporal shift can create false confidence in backtest results when the same logic fails in live trading.

core.when: When validating position state during order execution
core.action: validate position is finite during each order processing

consequence.description: Infinite or NaN positions cause arithmetic failures in size calculations and corrupt trade PnL records

core.when: When processing order records to build trade records
core.action: validate order size is greater than zero before creating trade records

consequence.description: Zero or negative order sizes in trade generation produce invalid trade records with corrupted size fields and incorrect PnL calculations

core.when: When validating order IDs in trade generation
core.action: verify order IDs come in ascending order per column during trade pairing

consequence.description: Non-ascending order IDs cause trade pairing to fail, producing incorrect entry/exit matches and corrupted PnL calculations

core.when: When implementing rolling performance metrics
core.action: align window to current timestep without including future data using i+1-window slicing

consequence.description: Rolling window must use past data only (current index excluded) to avoid look-ahead bias; using current or future data in rolling calculation inflates metric accuracy and produces misleading live trading signals

core.when: When passing price data to the portfolio simulator
core.action: Verify close price arrays have consistent datetime index and float64 dtype across each symbols

consequence.description: Portfolio simulation may produce incorrect trade records and position valuations when price arrays have mismatched indexes, causing financial calculation errors in PnL and returns

core.when: When passing indicator outputs to signal generation or portfolio simulation
core.action: Handle NaN values in indicator outputs that appear in initial lookback periods before passing to signal processing

consequence.description: Trading signals generated from NaN indicator values will produce false signals at the beginning of backtests, causing incorrect trade execution and inflated/deflated returns

core.when: When simulating portfolio with stop-loss or take-profit orders
core.action: Use the close price array from the same data source for both signal generation and order execution to verify consistent valuation

consequence.description: Stop-loss and take-profit orders will execute at incorrect prices when using different price arrays for signals versus execution, causing position sizing and PnL calculations to be inconsistent with actual trading

core.when: When processing signals and stop orders at the same bar
core.action: Execute stop signals BEFORE user-defined signals when they occur at the same time step

consequence.description: Look-ahead bias occurs when using closing price with stop orders - the stop signal must be evaluated first to avoid using future information

core.when: When using bshift (backward shift) function on signal or price arrays
core.action: Use bshift output as features for strategy generation without understanding it looks ahead into future information

consequence.description: Backtest equity curve exhibits look-ahead bias causing live trading returns to fall far below backtested results

core.when: When loading or using models and data in production backtesting pipelines
core.action: Assume the framework automatically binds model and data versions - version snapshot binding is not implemented and must be handled externally

consequence.description: Without version snapshot binding, production backtests may run with mismatched model-data versions, causing subtle result drift that is difficult to diagnose and potentially leading to incorrect trading decisions

core.when: When comparing volatility estimates from indicators with risk metrics
core.action: Use consistent DDOF (degrees of freedom) across each statistical calculations - indicators must use ddof=1 if metrics use ddof=1, or explicitly document when DDOF differs between indicator calculations and risk metric calculations

consequence.description: Indicators use ddof=0 (population variance) while metrics use ddof=1 (sample variance); Bollinger Bands use population std while Sharpe Ratio uses sample std, causing band widths and risk estimates to be calculated differently, making strategies appear more or less risky than actual

core.when: When implementing multi-agent trading systems with independent agent signals in backtesting
core.action: Assume the framework provides multi-agent decision consensus mechanisms — the framework does not implement this feature; conflicting agent signals will be processed without automated resolution

consequence.description: Without multi-agent consensus, conflicting agent signals result in unpredictable system behavior, potential race conditions in order generation, and backtest-live inconsistency when agents disagree

core.when: When implementing CLA, the golden section search tolerance is 1.0e-9
core.action: modify the golden section search tolerance from the hardcoded 1.0e-9 value

consequence.description: Relaxed tolerance produces less accurate Sharpe ratio maximization; stricter tolerance causes excessive iterations without meaningful improvement

core.when: When cleaning portfolio weights for output or discrete allocation
core.action: Treat weights with absolute value below 1e-4 as exactly zero to verify numerical stability

consequence.description: Near-zero weights cause unnecessary trading friction, precision errors in discrete allocation, and degraded Sharpe ratio calculations

core.when: When preparing covariance matrix inputs for quadratic optimization
core.action: Verify covariance matrix is positive semi-definite (PSD) via Cholesky decomposition before passing to quadratic optimizer — non-PSD matrices indicate numerical instability or data quality issues

consequence.description: Non-PSD covariance matrices cause Cholesky decomposition failures in quadratic optimizers, producing NaN/Inf portfolio weights that cannot be executed in live trading

core.when: When processing price data inputs for portfolio optimization or backtesting
core.action: Assume the framework automatically detects or validates data freshness — the framework does not implement stale data detection or cache TTL policies

consequence.description: Without stale data detection, outdated prices are used silently causing portfolio weights to be based on stale market data, leading to execution failures and tracking errors in live trading

core.when: When implementing custom objectives using the strategy pattern for portfolio optimization
core.action: Assume that custom objectives can be incrementally modified after initial solve - the optimizer does not support re-solving with modified objectives; design custom objectives to be self-contained and complete on first instantiation

consequence.description: Attempting to modify custom objectives after initial solve causes silent failures where the optimizer does not re-compute, leading to backtest results that do not reflect the intended objective changes and creating inconsistency between backtest and live trading

core.when: When parsing grid CSV data from broker exports in account query operations
core.action: Use broker-specific GRID_DTYPE mapping instead of pandas default dtype inference to correctly handle Chinese column headers and numeric format variations

consequence.description: Using pandas default dtype inference on broker CSV exports causes silent parsing errors and incorrect numeric interpretation due to inconsistent Chinese headers and decimal separators between broker data formats

core.when: When implementing authentication and session initialization for trading operations
core.action: Assume that random seed is automatically initialized or seeded by the framework — the framework does not implement comprehensive random seed initialization

consequence.description: Without explicit random seed initialization, reproducibility of backtest results and strategy simulations cannot be guaranteed across different runtime environments

core.when: When parsing broker grid data extracted via clipboard operations
core.action: Validate GRID_DTYPE mappings after broker software updates, as broker UI format changes will cause pandas parsing to fail silently or raise exceptions

consequence.description: Stale GRID_DTYPE mappings after broker updates cause silent parsing failures or exceptions, resulting in incomplete position data and incorrect order sizing in live trading

core.when: When implementing trade execution worker architecture
core.action: Use queue-based single-threaded processing to verify trades execute in strict FIFO order — do not implement multi-threaded or concurrent trade processing

consequence.description: Multi-threaded processing breaks FIFO ordering guarantee, causing trades to execute out of sequence which leads to incorrect position calculations and potential financial losses in live trading

core.when: When implementing or refactoring thread-based strategy tracking in the follower module
core.action: Verify strategy tracking threads are configured as daemon threads using setDaemon(True) before starting — threads must not block program exit when main thread completes

consequence.description: Non-daemon threads prevent clean process exit, causing backtest scripts and automated trading systems to hang indefinitely after completion; this blocks subsequent scheduled runs and causes resource leaks

core.when: When handling zero-amount token transfers during decoding
core.action: skip and ignore zero-amount transfers without creating events

consequence.description: Zero-amount transfers create noise in transaction histories and can cause divide-by-zero errors in PnL calculations; skipping them improves decoding performance and data quality

core.when: When handling matched asset movements
core.action: delete the matched pair when purging one side of the location

consequence.description: Deleting matched pairs incorrectly causes accounting imbalance and incorrect PnL attribution between exchange and blockchain events

core.when: When adding missing acquisitions for unsatisfied spends
core.action: only record missing acquisitions for non-fiat assets

consequence.description: Recording missing acquisitions for fiat assets causes incorrect cost basis calculations and distorts PnL reports for currency exchanges

core.when: When resolving assets for cost basis calculations
core.action: Call Asset.resolve() before using any asset in calculations; WETH must resolve to ETH for cost basis tracking

consequence.description: Unresolved assets cause MissingAcquisition errors and incorrect cost basis tracking, leading to incorrect PnL calculations and tax liability over/under-reporting

core.when: When implementing historical data retrieval for API layer
core.action: Implement timestamp-parameterized queries or snapshot mechanisms to fetch historical states; verify that historical data endpoints accept timestamp parameters and return point-in-time consistent data

consequence.description: Without proper point-in-time data retrieval, tax reports and audit trails use current balances for historical periods, causing incorrect tax calculations and non-compliance with audit requirements

core.when: When implementing multi-factor or statistical arbitrage strategies in backtesting
core.action: Assume the framework handles signal-execution timing synchronization — the framework does not implement latency modeling or execution delay compensation; signals are assumed to execute at exact generation timestamps

consequence.description: Without signal-execution timing handling, high-frequency statistical arbitrage strategies show unrealistic execution at stale prices; live trading misses signals or executes at prices 2-10 bps worse than backtested prices

core.when: When calculating position key for position mode switching scenarios
core.action: migrate existing positions to new key format when switching from ONEWAY to HEDGE mode or vice versa

consequence.description: Positions become inaccessible after mode switch because keys change format, causing position loss and incorrect PnL tracking

core.when: When handling executor position summaries with NaN values
core.action: Calculate quote amounts lazily when base amounts are valid and quote amounts are NaN

consequence.description: Using NaN quote amounts in PnL calculations produces incorrect position summaries, leading to wrong trading decisions

core.when: When configuring DCA executor simulator
core.action: set DCAMode.TAKER as the execution mode

consequence.description: NotImplementedError is raised at runtime when TAKER mode is used in DCAExecutorSimulator, preventing backtest from running

core.when: When configuring backtesting resolution
core.action: use a valid candle interval from the supported set (1s, 1m, 3m, 5m, 15m, 30m, 1h, 2h, 4h, 6h, 8h, 12h, 1d, 3d, 1w, 1M)

consequence.description: Backtest fails to initialize because the candle feed cannot be created for unsupported intervals

core.when: When executing backtest simulation loop
core.action: process market data row-by-row in chronological order using iterrows

consequence.description: Temporal ordering violation causes executors to receive prices out of sequence, corrupting PnL calculations and state tracking

core.when: When filtering historical candles for backtesting
core.action: drop NaN values after merging features with candle data to verify clean time-series

consequence.description: NaN values in features cause incorrect signal processing or executor state corruption during backtest simulation

core.when: When implementing performance tracking in executor orchestration
core.action: Replace in-memory caching with database writes on every tick — the framework relies on in-memory caching for performance during the tick loop and persists only on graceful shutdown

consequence.description: Adding database writes to the tick loop introduces ~10ms overhead per tick, causing backtest loop degradation and live trading latency spikes that exceed timing requirements

core.when: When processing extension loading logic that requires portfolio rebalancing
core.action: Assume the framework provides a rebalancing trigger mechanism — the framework does not implement automatic portfolio rebalancing triggers; positions continue without rebalancing until manual intervention

consequence.description: Without rebalancing triggers, portfolio allocations drift from target over time, causing unintended risk exposure that compounds silently in live trading

core.when: When implementing finite difference PDE solvers for option pricing
core.action: Assume the framework validates finite difference grid stability — the framework does not implement CFL (Courant-Friedrichs-Lewy) condition checks; unstable grids will produce divergent or NaN results

consequence.description: Without CFL validation, finite difference schemes may become numerically unstable, producing NaN values or wildly oscillating prices that corrupt backtest results

core.when: When configuring finite difference grids for PDE-based option pricing
core.action: Validate time step dt satisfies the CFL condition: dt <= dx² / (2D) for diffusion equations and dt <= dx / |v| for convection, where D is diffusion coefficient, dx is spatial step, and v is convection velocity; reject configurations failing this check

consequence.description: Failing to validate CFL before running finite difference schemes can produce numerical explosions that invalidate entire backtest runs and make results non-reproducible

core.when: When instantiating the data catalog
core.action: Enforce singleton pattern to prevent multiple catalog instances pointing to different data sources

consequence.description: Multiple catalog instances could cause inconsistent data queries, missing or duplicate data, and non-deterministic backtest results

core.when: When implementing time range generation for gap-filling
core.action: Request adjacent intervals to fill gaps rather than individual timestamps to reduce request overhead

consequence.description: Inefficient gap-filling would cause excessive data requests, longer backtest initialization times, and potential API rate limit issues

core.when: When handling data requests with time ranges
core.action: Verify request start timestamp does not exceed end timestamp before processing

consequence.description: Invalid time ranges would cause assertion errors or undefined behavior in data queries, crashing backtest runs

core.when: When using position purge functionality
core.action: preserve event ordering when reapplying fills for state reconstruction

consequence.description: Incorrect event ordering during position rebuild produces wrong realized PnL, quantity, and average price calculations, corrupting position state.

core.when: When implementing crash recovery
core.action: verify positions are correctly reconstructed from persisted fill events

consequence.description: Incomplete position reconstruction leaves positions in incorrect state, causing wrong PnL calculations and potentially triggering unintended trades.

core.when: When purging closed positions from the cache
core.action: purge positions that are still open

consequence.description: Purging open positions causes position state to become inconsistent with actual trading state, leading to incorrect PnL and risk calculations.

core.when: When managing single-currency accounts
core.action: verify exactly one currency balance exists in account state events

consequence.description: Single-currency accounts with multiple currency balances violate denomination consistency, causing unpredictable PnL and margin calculations

core.when: When writing Account balance and margin snapshots
core.action: Persist Account state snapshots to Cache to maintain balance history and margin utilization tracking

consequence.description: Account history is lost, preventing PnL reconciliation, margin breach investigation, and audit trail reconstruction

core.when: When processing spread instrument (combo) fills
core.action: Update portfolio positions for spread fills used only for order management purposes

consequence.description: Duplicate position updates for combo instruments cause incorrect PnL calculations and margin over-counting

core.when: When assigning position identifiers
core.action: Only create or modify positions through order fill processing — positions must never be created or modified outside of order fill events

consequence.description: Direct position manipulation bypasses the ExecutionEngine's order tracking, causing position state to diverge from actual order state and corrupting PnL calculations

core.when: When implementing conditional order logic or modifying the order state machine
core.action: Preserve the distinction between LOCAL states (Initialized, Emulated, Released) and VENUE states (Submitted, Accepted, etc.) — do not merge or flatten these state hierarchies

consequence.description: Flattening LOCAL and VENUE states breaks the emulation layer, causing conditional orders to submit prematurely without proper trigger validation, leading to incorrect order routing in backtesting and live trading

core.when: When running HFT strategy backtests that depend on reproducible results
core.action: Verify random seed (BD-044) covers each latency-affected components — verify no random operations exist outside the seeded scope when simulating HFT strategies

consequence.description: Non-deterministic HFT backtests cause strategies to pass or fail depending on random draws from LatencyModel, making edge cases unreproducible and backtest results unreliable for strategy validation

core.when: When processing large multi-instrument backtest data in streaming mode
core.action: Use deferred sorting for large datasets to achieve O(n) performance — never rely on incremental sorting during streaming ingestion which causes O(n²) degradation

consequence.description: Incremental sorting during streaming data ingestion causes O(n²) performance degradation on large multi-instrument backtests, making backtesting impractical for large historical datasets

core.when: When implementing or refactoring strategy initialization code that involves time-sensitive operations
core.action: Verify that the Actor's placeholder Clock has been properly replaced by the registered trading context Clock before executing any time-dependent logic

consequence.description: Time-sensitive operations executed with placeholder Clock produce incorrect timing behavior or silent failures, causing scheduled tasks to use wrong timestamps and backtest results to diverge from live execution

core.when: When implementing position calculation logic in backtesting or live trading
core.action: Use fill-based aggregation model where each fill updates an internal cost-bar — calculate position state from completed fills deterministically, never from real-time price feeds

consequence.description: Using streaming mark-to-market breaks broker parity; position values differ from broker statements, causing reconciliation failures and live trading account mismatches that are difficult to debug

core.when: When adding new instrument types to the trading system
core.action: Explicitly decide whether new instrument types belong in ENGINE_EXPIRING_INSTRUMENT_CLASSES whitelist for automatic expiration handling or require manual expiration processing; do not assume automatic inclusion or exclusion

consequence.description: Adding instruments without determining expiration handling causes incorrect behavior: non-expiring instruments processed through expiration logic or expiring instruments missing expiration, leading to stale positions and incorrect backtest results

core.when: When configuring LLM temperature parameters
core.action: Set temperature to 0.1 (very low) across each LLM adapters for deterministic, reproducible output

consequence.description: High temperature causes non-deterministic analyst outputs, making backtest results unreproducible and analysis signals inconsistent across repeated runs

core.when: When using covariate data in strategy signal generation during backtesting
core.action: Assume the framework handles covariate time availability constraints — the framework does not implement timestamp alignment validation for covariates; covariates with future timestamps relative to signal timestamps may be used without restriction

consequence.description: Without covariate time availability validation, look-ahead bias occurs where signals inadvertently use future covariate values, causing backtest returns to significantly exceed live trading performance

core.when: When implementing pairs trading or stateful signal logic
core.action: enforce state machine transitions to prevent re-emitting the same direction

consequence.description: Without state machine enforcement, the same directional signal is emitted repeatedly, causing excessive trading, commission waste, and portfolio construction conflicts

core.when: When expired insights exist for symbols with no active insights
core.action: Create flatten targets (quantity=0) for symbols with expired insights to close positions

consequence.description: Missing flatten targets for expired insights leaves orphaned positions open, causing unintended exposure and PnL leakage

core.when: When implementing risk management position state tracking
core.action: reset trailing state when position direction changes from long to short or vice versa

consequence.description: Using stale trailing state from a previous position direction would calculate drawdown incorrectly, potentially triggering premature liquidation or missing real drawdown

core.when: When implementing or refactoring the EMA cross alpha model
core.action: Use 12-period fast EMA and 26-period slow EMA for cross signals — do not change these parameters without separate validation evidence

consequence.description: Changing EMA periods alters signal timing and crossover frequency, causing strategies to generate different trading signals than validated in backtesting and potentially underperforming in live trading

core.when: When implementing pair selection correlation logic
core.action: Use Kendall tau correlation for pair selection — do not substitute Pearson or other methods

consequence.description: Using Pearson correlation instead of Kendall tau exposes pair selection to distortion from extreme values in financial returns, causing wrong pairs to be identified and strategies to fail in live trading

core.when: When settlement date equals a coupon payment date
core.action: Pay full coupon to the seller with accrued interest equaling the coupon amount

consequence.description: Incorrect coupon allocation on settlement date will violate market convention and cause disputes with counterparties

core.when: When calibrating equity implied volatility surfaces for arbitrage-free interpolation
core.action: Use SVI (Stochastic Volatility Inspired) parameterization with 5 parameters per maturity — do not use raw spline interpolation as it risks calendar spread arbitrage and produces unstable surfaces

consequence.description: Raw spline interpolation of volatility surfaces can produce arbitrage opportunities, causing Monte Carlo and tree models to price derivatives inconsistently and generate exploitable arbitrage profits in backtesting

core.when: When implementing financial transaction recording in the utilities stage
core.action: Assume the framework enforces double-entry bookkeeping integrity — this capability was identified as missing by audit checklist and is not implemented

consequence.description: Without double-entry bookkeeping integrity, debits and credits may become unbalanced, causing trial balance errors and potential regulatory capital miscalculations

core.when: When forecasting models use past observed covariates
core.action: Verify past covariate lags are negative integers (before current time) — past covariates represent historical data available at prediction time

consequence.description: Using non-negative or invalid past covariate lags causes look-ahead bias where the model uses future information not available at prediction time, invalidating forecast accuracy metrics

core.when: When evaluating model performance using MAPE or similar percentage-based metrics
core.action: Verify series values are non-zero and positive — MAPE division by actual values causes infinite or undefined metrics when series contains zeros

consequence.description: Percentage metrics produce undefined results (infinity, NaN) for zero-value observations, corrupting backtest results and making model comparison impossible

core.when: When configuring ARIMA model parameters in statistical forecasting
core.action: Verify the differencing parameter d accepts only non-negative integer values (0, 1, 2, ...) — reject float values, negative values, or fractional orders as they violate ARIMA specification and produce undefined model behavior

consequence.description: Non-integer differencing order causes the ARIMA model to fail during fitting or produce mathematically invalid results, making backtest equity curves unreliable for strategy evaluation

core.when: When using NLLScorer for anomaly detection scoring
core.action: Verify the NLLScorer has a fitted distribution before computing scores — the negative log-likelihood scoring requires fitted distribution parameters to quantify how anomalous observed values are

consequence.description: Computing NLL scores without a fitted distribution produces undefined anomaly scores, causing unreliable anomaly detection results in backtesting and live monitoring

core.when: When loading built-in datasets for backtesting or model training
core.action: Use dataset loaders that inherit from DatasetLoaderCSV and trigger on-demand download — do not assume datasets are bundled; handle download failures gracefully; verify dataset availability before use in critical pipelines

consequence.description: On-demand download failures cause pipeline interruptions when bundled datasets are expected but not present, leading to failed backtests or training runs without clear error messages

core.when: When implementing multi-step forecasting with sklearn models that have multioutput regression
core.action: Use per-horizon estimator pattern when model lacks native multioutput support — do not refactor to wrapper-based approach; train separate estimators for each forecast horizon to maintain consistent multi-step behavior

consequence.description: Changing to wrapper-based multioutput handling causes inconsistent behavior across different scikit-learn estimator types, potentially breaking backtest results for models that were trained with per-horizon pattern

core.when: When training sklearn-based forecasting models in backtesting
core.action: Generate lagged features from target and covariates at fit time — do not refactor to static pre-extracted features; lagged feature generation is fundamental for transforming time series into tabular format for regression

consequence.description: Moving lagged feature generation to static pre-processing breaks sklearn model compatibility and changes the temporal relationship between features and targets, causing backtest-live inconsistency

core.when: When implementing or modifying data transformation operations in backtesting
core.action: Change the copy=False parameter in transformer internal operations — transformers must maintain the copy=False contract to verify time series immutability and external reference validity

consequence.description: Changing copy=False to copy=True doubles memory usage for large time series operations, causing potential out-of-memory errors and significantly degrading backtest performance for high-frequency or large datasets

core.when: When implementing resample queries with the origin parameter in arcticdb
core.action: Document and validate the origin parameter choice ('epoch', 'start', 'end', or specific timestamp) before running queries — understand that origin directly affects bucket boundaries and can significantly change aggregation results

consequence.description: Incorrect origin parameter alignment causes bucket boundaries to shift, resulting in materially different aggregation results that corrupt downstream analysis and backtesting conclusions

core.when: When implementing timestamp handling in ArcticDB backtesting operations
core.action: Assume the framework supports timestamps outside datetime64[ns] resolution; each time indexes must be convertible to nanosecond precision as defined by _NS_DTYPE

consequence.description: Attempting to store or process timestamps with millisecond, second, or date resolution without conversion causes type mismatches and silent data truncation, producing incorrect backtest timestamps

core.when: When implementing streaming data processing in ArcticDB where finalize() transitions data from APPEND_DATA to APPEND_REF state
core.action: Skip, bypass, or optimize away the finalize() call — the two-phase commit pattern must complete before data becomes readable

consequence.description: Without finalize(), streams remain in APPEND_DATA state and reads can access incomplete data, causing silent data corruption in backtests and downstream analytics

core.when: When loading market data, CSV loader must accept multiple delimiter formats for flexibility
core.action: Parse CSV files using delimiters: comma, semicolon, tab, and space with token compression enabled

consequence.description: Data files created with different regional settings (European comma-decimal vs US period-decimal) fail to parse correctly, producing incorrect token counts

core.when: When computing credit migration files for visualization
core.action: generate credit migration output with pdf and cdf columns for each requested time step

consequence.description: Missing credit migration distributions prevents analysis of credit migration risk and regulatory stress testing

core.when: When writing netting set exposure CSVs
core.action: include EPE, ENE, PFE, and EEPE columns for each netting set ID

consequence.description: Incomplete exposure columns prevent proper netting set risk analysis and regulatory reporting

core.when: When writing scenario dump files for SIMM calculation
core.action: include each risk factor keys that are simulated in the scenario generator configuration

consequence.description: Missing risk factors in scenario dump causes incomplete SIMM calculation and incorrect regulatory capital requirements

core.when: When implementing discount factor to zero rate conversion for yield curve building
core.action: Apply log-linear discount-to-zero-rate conversion for maturities exceeding 10 years — use alternative interpolation methods (cubic spline) for long-dated instruments where curvature is high

consequence.description: Using log-linear approximation for maturities beyond 10 years causes accuracy degradation that introduces material errors in CSA-based exposure calculations, resulting in incorrect XVA valuations and potential regulatory capital miscalculations

core.when: When calibrating interest rate models (e.g., Hull-White) to swaption volatility surface
core.action: Use Levenberg-Marquardt optimization algorithm combining Gauss-Newton and gradient descent methods; verify initial parameters are within convergence radius

consequence.description: Incorrect optimization algorithm selection causes convergence failures or poor calibration quality, producing inaccurate risk factors that invalidate regulatory capital calculations

core.when: When implementing model calibration optimization loops
core.action: Set explicit termination criteria: max iterations=1000, max stationary state=10, function tolerance=1e-8, gradient tolerance=1e-8, x-tolerance=1e-8 to verify reproducibility

consequence.description: Missing or incorrect termination criteria causes infinite loops or premature convergence, degrading calibration quality and breaking reproducibility required for audit compliance

core.when: When implementing NPL portfolio data processing in the analytics engine
core.action: Assume the framework automatically validates NPL portfolio EBA field completeness — the framework does not implement EBA field validation for NPL data; missing fields will cause silent data corruption in downstream analytics

consequence.description: Without explicit EBA field validation, NPL portfolio records with missing required fields propagate through the analytics pipeline, causing incorrect regulatory reporting and risk calculations

core.when: When processing NPL portfolio data in the analytics engine
core.action: Implement explicit EBA field validation that checks each required fields are populated before processing, and fail with explicit error if any required field is missing or null

consequence.description: Without explicit field validation, NPL portfolio records with incomplete EBA fields silently propagate through the system, corrupting downstream risk calculations and regulatory reports

core.when: When preparing Fama-French factors and risk-free rate for OLS regression
core.action: divide percentage-formatted FF factors and risk-free rate by 100 before using in regression

consequence.description: FF factors stored as percentages (e.g., 5.2) must be converted to decimals (0.052); using raw percentages produces coefficients scaled by 100x and invalid inference

core.when: When implementing stock data merging logic in the returns.calculation stage
core.action: Use OUTER JOIN when merging component stock data to preserve each observations from each stock — do not use INNER JOIN as it loses data when trading dates misalign

consequence.description: Inner join silently drops observations where dates don't align, reducing sample size unpredictably and causing survivorship bias in factor calculations

core.when: When processing data in production data collection pipeline
core.action: Assume the framework handles stale data detection and automatic expiry — this capability is confirmed absent; the framework does not validate data freshness or expire outdated records

consequence.description: Using stale data in regression analysis produces incorrect coefficients based on outdated market conditions, leading to strategies with wrong factor loadings and significant capital losses in live trading

core.when: When implementing or modifying any code involving random number generation
core.action: Assume each random number generators have reproducible seeds by default — random seed coverage is confirmed incomplete; numpy.random, random module, and other RNG calls may lack explicit seed configuration

consequence.description: Using non-reproducible RNG seeds causes backtest results to vary between runs, making it impossible to reproduce strategy performance or verify code changes against established benchmarks

core.when: When estimating intraday positions from transactions
core.action: Apply shift(1) to EOD positions before adding intraday transaction deltas

consequence.description: Without shift(1), positions at day-start are contaminated with end-of-day values, causing look-ahead bias that makes intraday-adjusted results unrepresentative of actual peak-exposure moments.

core.when: When calculating daily transaction volume
core.action: Normalize transaction timestamps to date level using groupby(level=0).sum()

consequence.description: Non-normalized timestamps cause intraday trades to be incorrectly aggregated across date boundaries, producing inaccurate transaction volume calculations for slippage and turnover.

core.when: When calculating long and short allocations
core.action: Define net liquidation as sum of longs plus shorts plus cash when computing allocation percentages

consequence.description: Net liquidation denominator excluding cash would misrepresent the true portfolio size, causing long/short allocations to not sum to 100% and leading to incorrect leverage calculations

core.when: When identifying short positions
core.action: Filter for negative position values only when computing short allocations

consequence.description: Including positive values in short sum would inflate the short allocation percentage, misrepresenting the portfolio's actual short exposure and leverage characteristics

core.when: When implementing capacity analysis with market data, the transactions index MUST be normalized to date level and grouped by symbol and day
core.action: Group transactions by ['symbol', pd.Grouper(freq='D')] and sum amounts to compute daily transaction totals per security

consequence.description: Mismatched grouping will cause transaction totals to be incorrectly attributed to wrong dates or symbols, corrupting liquidity analysis results

core.when: When extracting round trips from transactions DataFrame
core.action: Verify transactions DataFrame contains required columns: amount, price, symbol

consequence.description: Missing required columns causes KeyError exceptions during round trip extraction, breaking PnL calculation and duration computation

core.when: When processing transactions with negative prices
core.action: Include transactions with negative prices in round trip calculations

consequence.description: Negative prices create invalid PnL calculations and corrupt round trip attribution by producing mathematically impossible gains/losses

core.when: When processing transactions with zero transacted shares
core.action: Calculate VWAP from a group with zero total transacted amount

consequence.description: Division by zero produces NaN values that propagate through PnL calculations, corrupting downstream statistics and visualizations

core.when: When using FIFO matching algorithm for round trip pairing
core.action: Maintain order preservation using deque data structure for price stack

consequence.description: Non-FIFO matching violates accounting principle of first-in-first-out, producing incorrect PnL attribution and invalid round trip boundaries

core.when: When processing transactions across multiple symbols
core.action: Group transactions by symbol before applying FIFO matching

consequence.description: Cross-symbol matching produces invalid round trips that pair shares of different securities, generating meaningless PnL and duration statistics

core.when: When calculating VWAP for grouped transactions
core.action: Compute VWAP as sum of (amount * price) divided by sum of amount

consequence.description: Incorrect VWAP formula produces wrong average prices for merged transactions, distorting PnL calculations for round trips spanning multiple trades

core.when: When computing drawdown metrics from return series
core.action: correctly identify peak, valley, and recovery dates using the underwater DataFrame algorithm

consequence.description: Drawdown table will report incorrect peak, valley, and recovery dates, causing misleading risk analysis for trading strategies

core.when: When computing rolling volatility and Sharpe ratio
core.action: annualize volatility using sqrt(APPROX_BDAYS_PER_YEAR) where APPROX_BDAYS_PER_YEAR equals 252

consequence.description: Annualized volatility and Sharpe ratio will be incorrectly scaled if using calendar days instead of trading days

core.when: When scaling slippage penalties for different capital bases
core.action: Scale both trade size numerator and portfolio value denominator by the same multiplier

consequence.description: Scaling only the trade size without adjusting portfolio value produces inconsistent slippage penalties that misrepresent market impact at different capital levels

core.when: When identifying low-liquidity transactions
core.action: Calculate bar consumption as transaction amount divided by daily volume

consequence.description: Incorrect bar consumption calculation causes misidentification of low-liquidity positions, hiding potential market impact issues from portfolio managers

core.when: When providing positions DataFrame to any pyfolio function
core.action: Include a 'cash' column representing net capital not invested in securities

consequence.description: Functions that compute portfolio totals (turnover, leverage, etc.) sum all columns including cash, causing incorrect calculations when cash column is missing

core.when: When providing transactions DataFrame to any pyfolio function
core.action: Include columns: amount, price, symbol with datetime index

consequence.description: Transaction processing functions (get_txn_vol, adjust_returns_for_slippage, extract_round_trips) access these specific columns by name, causing KeyError when columns are missing or using different names

core.when: When annualizing returns or computing Sharpe ratio
core.action: Use APPROX_BDAYS_PER_YEAR=252 for daily returns, APPROX_BDAYS_PER_MONTH=21 for approximations

consequence.description: Using calendar days instead of trading days inflates annualized metrics by approximately 15%, causing misleading performance comparisons

core.when: When processing EOD positions for starting capital calculation
core.action: Verify that the 1-day position shift aligns with your data pipeline's timestamp convention; if positions are reported at start-of-day, remove the shift

consequence.description: Incorrect position timestamp alignment causes first-day returns to be calculated against wrong starting capital; look-ahead bias may be introduced if positions and capital are misaligned by a day

core.when: When implementing or modifying position data schema for backtesting
core.action: Verify the cash column is always present in positions DataFrame and properly populated; for short positions, verify cash contribution is correctly calculated using short proceeds accounting

consequence.description: Missing cash column causes cascading NaN propagation through allocation calculations and position analysis, producing incorrect portfolio weights that silently distort backtest results
