core.when: When computing Sortino ratio for risk-adjusted performance
core.action: Verify Sortino ratio implementation uses downside deviation (clipping returns at threshold) as denominator, NOT full standard deviation; threshold should be 0 for simple cases or configurable target rate

consequence.description: Using full standard deviation for Sortino ratio misrepresents investor preferences by penalizing upside volatility equally, producing misleading risk-adjusted performance metrics that cannot be compared with Sharpe ratio

core.when: When implementing return adjustment logic with factor parameter
core.action: Implement defensive copy before modification when factor=0 to prevent mutation of original returns array; or verify _adjust_returns always returns a copy regardless of factor value

consequence.description: No-copy mutation when factor=0 causes silent data corruption where original returns array is modified, propagating incorrect values through nanmean/nanstd and corrupting all downstream metrics (Sharpe, Sortino, max_drawdown, beta) without raising errors

core.when: When implementing NaN-handling functions for return calculations
core.action: Verify NaN-handling fallback paths produce identical results to bottleneck implementations; add assertions comparing outputs for sample data to catch silent divergence

consequence.description: Silent divergence between bottleneck and numpy fallback paths causes inconsistent metrics across environments, where backtests run differently depending on installed dependencies

core.when: When implementing standard deviation calculations for risk metrics
core.action: Verify ddof=1 is consistently used in each standard deviation calculations for sample variance estimation, regardless of whether bottleneck or numpy path is used

consequence.description: Inconsistent ddof values between bottleneck and numpy paths cause silent accuracy divergence where risk metrics differ between environments, violating correctness guarantees for backtest reproducibility

core.when: When fetching market data from external data sources
core.action: Implement fallback mechanism when data fetch returns None

consequence.description: Network failures, API errors, or missing credentials cause fetch_market to return None; without fallback, the entire backtest fails without generating any results

core.when: When computing discrete trading signals from technical indicators
core.action: output exclusively +1 (long), -1 (short), or NaN (flat) values

consequence.description: Signal values outside {+1, -1, NaN} will cause incorrect position sizing in the backtest engine, as the PnL calculation at backtestengine.py:201-216 assumes symmetric long/short encoding

core.when: When implementing indicator-based signal generation
core.action: introduce inherent 1-period lag through rolling window or shift operations

consequence.description: Signals generated without lag will exhibit look-ahead bias, causing live trading returns to fall far below backtested results because the strategy would have traded on information not yet available

core.when: When implementing total return index construction with multiplicative cumulation
core.action: Initialize TRI at base value 100 and compound returns forward using cumprod

consequence.description: Additive cumulation (cumsum) allows negative cumulative returns to break the index chain, causing TRI values to become meaningless for P&L calculation and potentially producing misleading backtest results

core.when: When joining TRI series with different currency pairs
core.action: Use outer join to preserve each dates across currency pairs

consequence.description: Inner join drops dates where only some pairs have data, creating gaps in multi-currency portfolio TRI and causing signal generation to skip valid trading days

core.when: When handling missing deposit rate data at start of TRI series
core.action: Forward-fill carry data and backward-fill only old data gaps, never forward-fill spot prices

consequence.description: Forward-filling spot prices creates look-ahead bias where future prices are used to calculate historical returns, invalidating all TRI-based backtest results

core.when: When building an FX volatility surface from market quotes
core.action: Use CLARK5 interpolation function type for smoother vol surface without arbitrage

consequence.description: Using BBG interpolation may produce jagged vol surfaces with potential arbitrage violations, causing incorrect option pricing and PnL calculation errors

core.when: When implementing volatility targeting with rolling window periods
core.action: account for NaN values in the first N periods before the rolling window completes

consequence.description: Volatility-adjusted leverage will be NaN for the initial periods equal to the rolling window (vol_periods), causing backtest P&L series to contain NaN values for warmup periods and potentially causing downstream calculation errors in portfolio aggregation

core.when: When implementing sensitivity analysis with parameter sweeps
core.action: reset trading model parameters after completing the sweep

consequence.description: Without parameter reset, subsequent analyses use incorrectly overridden parameter values, causing corrupted P&L reports and invalid Sharpe ratios across model runs

core.when: When configuring chart rendering engine
core.action: specify a valid chart engine from the supported set

consequence.description: Invalid engine name causes chart generation failure, blocking all plot outputs including Sharpe ratios and drawdown visualizations

core.when: When implementing signal transitions between directional positions
core.action: Use explicit zero assignment for neutral signals — do not leave signal value undefined or rely on implicit zero-crossing behavior during transitions

consequence.description: Leaving signal transitions undefined creates ambiguity that can introduce silent directional bias, causing incorrect position assignments in backtesting and misalignment with live trading execution

core.when: When constructing spot curves for FX carry calculations
core.action: Use overnight (ON) deposit tenor as the default starting point for spot curve construction — ON deposits are the most liquid tenor representing true overnight carry cost for position funding; override only for emerging market currencies with illiquid ON markets

consequence.description: Using 1W or 1M deposit as default adds complexity and less liquidity to carry interpretation, causing carry calculation errors that misrepresent true funding costs for FX positions

core.when: When implementing rolling technical indicators in signal generation
core.action: Set first n-1 periods to NaN to create warmup periods equal to indicator period — verify rolling indicators require full lookback window before generating signals

consequence.description: Without NaN warmup periods, rolling indicators generate unstable signals from incomplete history, causing look-ahead bias where signals use future information not available at signal time

core.when: When implementing SMA or other TA-Lib functions to accept pandas Series input
core.action: Return pandas.Series with the same index preserved from the input Series

consequence.description: Breaking pandas index preservation causes downstream code to lose temporal alignment, leading to incorrect backtest results and misaligned signal generation

core.when: When calculating indicators that depend on dynamic lookback periods (e.g., SMA, EMA, RSI with varying timeperiod parameters)
core.action: Verify lookback computation correctly accounts for endIdx as length-1 convention; verify the number of NaN values prepended matches the dynamic lookback derived from parameters (e.g., timeperiod=200 requires 199 prior bars lookback); validate output array length equals input length and valid results start at correct index

consequence.description: Off-by-one errors in lookback calculation cause output arrays with wrong length, incorrect NaN count in lookback period, and valid results starting at wrong index; for high-period indicators like SMA(200), this propagates to significant data misalignment in backtesting and live trading

core.when: When implementing or refactoring concurrent execution of shared Function instances
core.action: Use threading.local() for stateful computation buffers to isolate per-thread state; do NOT remove thread-local storage or replace with shared mutable state

consequence.description: Removing thread-local storage causes data races when multiple threads share Function instances concurrently; computed indicators from one thread bleed into another, producing incorrect signals and corrupting backtest results

core.when: When validating TA-Lib library installation completeness
core.action: Verify that the function count matches the expected count to detect incomplete TA-Lib installations early — do not skip function count validation as it allows broken installations to go undetected

consequence.description: Skipping function count validation allows incomplete TA-Lib installations to go undetected until specific functions are called, causing cryptic runtime errors during live trading when missing functions are accessed

core.when: When modifying the TA-Lib initialization lifecycle
core.action: Replace automatic initialization on import with lazy initialization — the C library must be initialized exactly once per process before any function calls

consequence.description: Switching to lazy initialization shifts initialization costs to the first function call and may cause cryptic C-level errors if TA-Lib functions are called before the library is ready, breaking user expectations in backtesting workflows

core.when: When implementing the TA-Lib Python API
core.action: Provide both Function API (stateless) and Abstract API (stateful) interfaces, sharing the same underlying C implementation for correctness and performance

consequence.description: Removing either the stateless or stateful API breaks existing user workflows — batch research relies on stateless calls while live trading relies on stateful streaming interfaces

core.when: When implementing or refactoring Abstract Function classes that maintain internal state
core.action: Use threading.local() for per-thread state isolation — each thread must maintain independent state arrays without sharing or locking

consequence.description: Without threading.local() isolation, concurrent threads sharing Abstract Function state will cause race conditions on internal state arrays, producing incorrect indicator values and non-deterministic backtest results

core.when: When configuring TA-Lib compatibility mode for indicator calculations
core.action: Set compatibility mode to 0 (default TA-Lib calculation behavior) — must not set to mode 1 or other alternative calculation methods

consequence.description: Setting compatibility mode to non-zero values causes TA-Lib functions to produce slightly different results for edge cases, breaking consistency with standard TA-Lib behavior and causing backtest-live discrepancies

core.when: When implementing or refactoring thread-safe data handling in TA-Lib multi-threaded usage
core.action: Deep copy input data before any manipulation to verify each thread operates on an independent copy — do not remove or replace deep copying with alternative synchronization that assumes shared-data safety

consequence.description: Removing deep copy causes race conditions when multiple threads access shared data structures, leading to corrupted intermediate calculations and non-deterministic backtest results across runs

core.when: When configuring throttling behavior for NBBO aggregation
core.action: Verify that the 60-second throttle window matches strategy requirements — for intraday or high-frequency strategies, explicitly configure a shorter window or disable throttling to capture each price updates

consequence.description: Strategies relying on fine-grained intraday signals silently lose all but one update per 60-second window, creating material divergence between backtested and live trading performance that accumulates over time

core.when: When implementing historical candle retrieval for OKX exchange
core.action: Verify end timestamp is exclusive (add 1ms) and start timestamp is inclusive (subtract 1ms) to prevent duplicate candle boundaries from overlapping time ranges

consequence.description: Incorrect timestamp boundaries cause duplicate candle data at query edges, inflating backtest counts and distorting historical analysis results

core.when: When implementing pagination logic for Binance REST historical trade queries
core.action: Verify pagination handles batches of 1000 trades maximum per request, implementing cursor-based pagination for queries spanning more than 1000 trades

consequence.description: Batches exceeding 1000 trades per request will trigger API errors or truncated responses, causing historical data gaps and incomplete backtesting datasets

core.when: When configuring candle interval for data aggregation across different exchanges
core.action: Verify that '1m' candle interval is natively supported by the target exchange API; if not supported, implement client-side aggregation or use an alternative supported interval

consequence.description: Assuming universal 1-minute candle support causes silent data gaps for exchanges without native 1m candles, corrupting historical analysis and backtesting results

core.when: When making any market-specific API call
core.action: Call loadMarkets() before any market-specific API call to verify the markets{} dictionary is populated, because lazy loading creates temporal coupling that causes KeyError or incorrect order sizing without prior loading

consequence.description: Calling market-specific APIs without loadMarkets() causes incomplete data returns or KeyError exceptions, making backtests fail or produce incorrect results due to missing market metadata

core.when: When implementing or using any optimization routine in the framework
core.action: Assume optimization routines will detect or handle convergence automatically without explicit convergence criteria configuration

consequence.description: Optimizers without explicit convergence criteria may run indefinitely, converge to incorrect local minima, or terminate prematurely, producing invalid strategy parameters that fail in live trading

core.when: When configuring any optimization algorithm (genetic algorithms, gradient descent, grid search)
core.action: Define explicit convergence criteria including: maximum iterations, minimum improvement threshold (e.g., 1e-6), maximum runtime, and validation checks; log convergence status after each optimization run

consequence.description: Without explicit convergence criteria, optimization may produce overfitted parameters that perform well on historical data but fail in live trading due to insufficient sampling of the parameter space

core.when: When implementing any randomized behavior in the SEC EDGAR download pipeline
core.action: Assume the framework handles random seed configuration for reproducible downloads — the framework does not implement random seed management, leading to non-deterministic download sequences across runs

consequence.description: Without random seed management, download sequences vary between runs causing inconsistent file ordering, potential duplicate downloads, and non-reproducible audit trails that fail regulatory compliance requirements

core.when: When constructing bar output values
core.action: verify OHLC columns (open, high, low, close) contain no NaN values

consequence.description: NaN values in OHLC columns propagate to downstream feature engineering, causing model training failures and incorrect backtest results

core.when: When implementing triple barrier labeling logic
core.action: Verify barrier touch time t1 is always greater than or equal to the event timestamp

consequence.description: If t1 is less than the event timestamp, the triple barrier labels will exhibit temporal causality violation, causing the ML model to learn from future information and produce misleading backtest results that cannot be replicated in live trading

core.when: When computing sample weights with time decay
core.action: produce negative weight values

consequence.description: Negative weights cause undefined behavior in sklearn classifiers, leading to invalid training outcomes or runtime exceptions when sample_weight is passed to fit methods

core.when: When time is constrained and quick results are needed
core.action: skip clustered feature importance analysis with correlated features

consequence.description: Skipping clustered_subsets with correlated features causes misleading importance rankings that lead to poor feature selection and degraded model performance in live trading

core.when: When requiring reproducible synthetic data generation
core.action: Set random seed before calling generation functions to verify reproducibility

consequence.description: Without fixed random seed, generated correlation matrices and time series vary between runs, preventing reproducible backtesting and simulation results

core.when: When bootstrapping time series data for sampling variability estimation
core.action: Preserve row integrity during bootstrapping to maintain temporal structure and autocorrelation within observations, using row-level sampling with replacement

consequence.description: Shuffling observations during bootstrap destroys temporal dependencies, causing look-ahead bias and invalid statistical inference for time series strategies

core.when: When setting up experiment tracking for strategy development
core.action: Implement an immutable audit log using content-addressable storage or append-only databases to capture every parameter modification, execution timestamp, and result hash — enable full reproducibility verification

consequence.description: Without immutable audit trails, performance changes cannot be attributed to specific parameter modifications, and regulatory compliance requirements for strategy auditability cannot be met

core.when: When interpreting ML model predictions using partial dependence-based fingerprinting
core.action: Verify partial dependence assumptions: verify feature independence when marginalizing, account for computational cost scaling as O(n × p), and use subgroup-specific PD rather than global PD when data exhibits heterogeneity across market regimes or asset classes

consequence.description: Partial dependence fingerprinting assumes feature independence which is violated in correlated feature sets, producing misleading feature importance rankings that cause misallocated risk management resources and failed regulatory explanations

core.when: When implementing environment step function
core.action: process SELL actions before BUY actions to prevent using same-tick cash from sells to fund buys

consequence.description: Agent can buy stocks using cash from sells on the same tick, violating T+1 settlement constraints and inflating backtest returns

core.when: When calculating execution price with market impact
core.action: subtract temporary impact from current price to get execution price

consequence.description: Incorrect impact subtraction causes fill prices to be calculated wrong, corrupting implementation shortfall and PnL calculations throughout the simulation

core.when: When computing technical indicators from price data
core.action: Apply shift(1) or equivalent lag to price-based indicators before using them as state features

consequence.description: Technical indicators computed on the current candle's close price create look-ahead bias — the agent sees information not yet available at decision time, inflating backtest returns and causing live underperformance

core.when: When executing the training-testing-trading pipeline
core.action: Verify training data precedes testing data in time, with no temporal overlap — training period end_date must be strictly less than testing period start_date

consequence.description: Temporal overlap between training and testing causes information leakage — the agent's policy has been trained on data it will be evaluated on, producing artificially inflated backtest results

core.when: When handling missing features at time=-1 in observation preprocessing
core.action: Fill missing features at time=-1 with zeros — do not use forward-fill (propagates stale values) or backward-fill (leaks future information)

consequence.description: Forward-fill propagates stale values causing model to see outdated information; backward-fill leaks future prices causing look-ahead bias in backtests

core.when: When implementing test evaluation logic for the execution environment
core.action: Process test instruments in sequential order as defined by the test sampler — do not introduce random shuffling, parallel processing, or non-deterministic ordering during test evaluation

consequence.description: Non-deterministic test ordering causes backtest results to vary across runs, making it impossible to reproduce published performance metrics or compare model improvements reliably

core.when: When modifying the FX trading environment action space
core.action: Preserve discrete action encoding {0:buy, 1:sell, 2:nothing} with fractional part usage for profit-taking behavior

consequence.description: Expanding to continuous action space or hierarchical decomposition fundamentally changes strategy representation, corrupting trained agent behavior and causing misalignment between trained policies and expected trading outcomes

core.when: When implementing linkage method selection
core.action: prefer DBHT linkage for deterministic results and preserved correlation structure

consequence.description: Scipy linkage methods with random tie-breaking produce non-reproducible dendrograms across runs; DBHT provides deterministic alternative essential for reproducible backtesting

core.when: When implementing data persistence or portfolio state management
core.action: Assume the framework provides versioned write operations or snapshot semantics for portfolio state changes - the framework does not implement these capabilities

consequence.description: Assuming versioned write semantics when the framework lacks them causes audit failures, inability to rollback portfolio state, and production reliability issues in live trading environments

core.when: When calculating within-period risk metrics like quarterly VaR
core.action: Use MultiIndex with MULTI_PERIOD_INDEX_LEVELS=2 to enable period-day hierarchical calculations

consequence.description: Without MultiIndex structure, within-period risk aggregation would be impossible, breaking VaR and drawdown calculations for sub-periods

core.when: When implementing subscription tier enforcement and rate limiting
core.action: Verify plan detection (BD-001), premium gating (BD-047), and sleep timer (BD-048) operate independently with explicit fallback mechanisms — if plan detection fails, default to most restrictive tier rather than propagating the failure

consequence.description: Cascading failures in the tier enforcement chain cause incorrect rate limiting (either too permissive or too restrictive) and data access violations, breaking both performance and compliance requirements simultaneously

core.when: When implementing or modifying the caching layer for data persistence
core.action: Maintain separate pickle serialization strategies: config data must use python pickle for flexibility, financial data must use pandas pickle for cross-version compatibility — do not interchange the two

consequence.description: Using the wrong pickle type causes silent deserialization failures when cached data is loaded in different pandas versions, leading to missing or corrupted financial metrics that produce incorrect backtest results without obvious errors

core.when: When computing survival rates from cumulative PD
core.action: Verify sR[k] = 1.0 - cPD[k] for each periods k to verify probability conservation

consequence.description: Violating the constraint sR + cPD = 1 means probability mass is not conserved. At period T with certainty of outcome (default or survival), the probabilities must sum to exactly 1.

core.when: When implementing or validating a transition matrix in the system
core.action: Verify each row sums equal 1.0 within the specified accuracy tolerance (default 1e-3)

consequence.description: Invalid transition matrices produce incorrect probability calculations, leading to flawed credit risk assessments and regulatory non-compliance

core.when: When calling Aalen-Johansen fit() method for duration-based estimation
core.action: Pre-sort input data in ascending time order before passing to the estimator

consequence.description: Unsorted data causes incorrect observation time extraction and produces invalid transition probability estimates with look-ahead bias

core.when: When computing forward returns
core.action: use prices that reflect entry price AFTER factor observation (not prices used in factor computation)

consequence.description: Using factor-computed prices for forward returns introduces look-ahead bias; factor should be observed at timestamp N while forward return starts from N+1

core.when: When computing factor returns for a demeaned (dollar-neutral) portfolio
core.action: verify long and short weights sum to equal magnitude so that portfolio returns sum to approximately zero

consequence.description: Asymmetric long-short weights cause unintended market exposure, violating the dollar-neutral assumption and producing misleading backtest results that cannot be replicated in live trading

core.when: When computing forward returns from price data
core.action: use shift(-period) to correctly align future prices with current factor values

consequence.description: Using shift(period) instead of shift(-period) introduces look-ahead bias, causing future information to be used in historical analysis and producing backtests that cannot be replicated in live trading

core.when: When inferring trading calendar from factor and prices index overlap
core.action: Verify that the inferred trading calendar correctly captures weekdays and holidays for the specific market — check for unexpected data gaps that may indicate calendar inference errors, and explicitly specify calendar parameter when accuracy is critical

consequence.description: Calendar inference errors cause misalignment between factor signals and price data on non-trading days, leading to incorrect return calculations and potentially excluding valid trading signals or including invalid ones

core.when: When calculating equal-weighted portfolio returns in performance analysis
core.action: Use median for cross-sectional demeaning in equal-weighted portfolio calculations instead of mean — this provides robustness to outlier constituent returns

consequence.description: Using mean for demeaning makes equal-weight returns sensitive to extreme constituent returns, causing backtest results to diverge from live trading where outlier positions have disproportionate impact on portfolio performance

core.when: When implementing data ingestion and storage logic in the framework
core.action: Assume the framework handles stale data detection and expiry policy — stale data detection and expiry policy are not implemented in the current codebase

consequence.description: Without stale data detection, the framework may process outdated market data causing strategies to trade on expired information, leading to significant financial losses in live trading while backtest results remain falsely confident

core.when: When implementing any stochastic components (Monte Carlo, bootstrapping, randomization) in the framework
core.action: Assume the framework has consistent random seed management across each components — random seed handling is not uniformly covered in the current implementation

consequence.description: Without explicit random seed coverage, backtest results become non-reproducible across runs, causing different equity curves for the same strategy and making it impossible to validate strategy performance or compare modifications reliably

core.when: When structuring Feature DataFrame for multi-ticker trading
core.action: Maintain (ticker, date) MultiIndex structure on output DataFrame

consequence.description: Downstream stages (prediction_modeling, backtesting) expect MultiIndex (ticker, date) for proper train/test temporal splits. Flattening or renaming index levels causes misalignment between predictions and price data, producing incorrect trading signals and corrupted backtest results.

core.when: When implementing cross-validation for financial time series data
core.action: use purged walking forward split with embargo gap between train and test

consequence.description: Without purge gap, overlapping outcomes between training and test periods contaminate validation results, producing artificially inflated performance metrics

core.when: When training ML models on financial time series
core.action: set shuffle=False as default to maintain temporal validity

consequence.description: Random shuffling destroys temporal ordering, allowing future data points to appear before past data points in training, causing catastrophic look-ahead bias

core.when: When integrating DSR into the strategy selection workflow
core.action: Apply DSR correction after each strategy testing is complete and before final selection

consequence.description: Applying DSR during testing (rather than after) changes the selection criteria mid-experiment, invalidating the statistical properties of both the raw and deflated Sharpe ratios

core.when: When passing scraped HTML data to market data ingestion
core.action: Parse HTML into structured CSV or HDF5 files with consistent column naming conventions

consequence.description: Unstructured data may cause parsing failures downstream, leading to incomplete price series and incorrect backtest results

core.when: When training ML models on historical financial data
core.action: Use point-in-time (PIT) data that reflects only information available at each historical date — adjusted prices and fundamental data must be PIT-corrected

consequence.description: Training on forward-filled or revised data creates look-ahead bias, causing models to fail in live trading when using only available information

core.when: When selecting models based on alpha signal evaluation using financial returns
core.action: Use Spearman rank correlation for model selection — do not use Pearson correlation which assumes normality and linearity and is less robust for returns

consequence.description: Pearson correlation on non-normal financial returns can produce misleading correlation estimates, causing model selection to favor strategies with spurious correlations that fail in live trading

core.when: When implementing normalized oscillator indicators with 0-100 range
core.action: use fillna value=50 to fill NaN values with the neutral midpoint of the oscillator range

consequence.description: Using other fillna values would misrepresent indicator state during warmup periods, potentially causing false overbought/oversold signals during backtesting

core.when: When returning computed indicator Series from result methods
core.action: preserve the input Series index object to maintain temporal alignment in downstream calculations

consequence.description: Creating a new Series with reindexed or reset index breaks temporal alignment with original price data, causing look-ahead bias or misaligned signals in trading strategies

core.when: When comparing outputs between class and functional interfaces
core.action: expect wrapper default parameters to match class defaults

consequence.description: Using default parameters with rsi() will produce different results than RSIIndicator(), leading to incorrect backtesting or trading signal generation

core.when: When passing internal pandas.Series from _run() computation to wrapper method accessors
core.action: return Series that preserve the original close price index for temporal alignment

consequence.description: If returned indicator Series lose the original time index, downstream feature aggregation will misalign indicators with each other and with price data, causing incorrect backtest results

core.when: When processing timestamped financial data in the data input stage
core.action: Assume processing time equals as-of time — these represent fundamentally different concepts: as-of time is when an event occurred, processing time is when data was recorded or ingested

consequence.description: Confusing as-of time with processing time causes timestamp-based joins and historical reconstruction to produce incorrect data associations, corrupting backtest signals and breaking audit trails

core.when: When implementing or refactoring shift operations in return and ROC calculations
core.action: Change the hardcoded shift(1) lookback period in return calculations — shift(1) is hardcoded across 18+ locations and conflicts with multi-period return definitions (Daily Return, Log Return, ROC)

consequence.description: Changing shift(1) to make it configurable breaks the documented flexibility; users cannot compute multi-period returns as documented when shift() is modified, causing backtest-live inconsistency

core.when: When loading models or datasets for strategy execution
core.action: Assume the framework automatically binds and tracks model versions to data versions — the framework does not implement version snapshot binding between models and data

consequence.description: Without model-data version binding, running a model trained on older data with newer market conditions may produce unpredictable results, and it becomes impossible to reproduce specific backtest runs when model or data versions change

core.when: When configuring Monte Carlo pricing engines for exotic options
core.action: Provide a positive integer seed parameter to the Monte Carlo engine constructor; validate that seed > 0 before instantiation

consequence.description: Zero or negative seed values cause exception during random path generation; strategies relying on Monte Carlo pricing fail to produce results, breaking backtesting reproducibility

core.when: When attaching a stochastic process to a pricing engine in quantitative finance code
core.action: Verify process and engine compatibility before attachment; mismatched pairs (e.g., lognormal process with non-lognormal engine) will throw exception at runtime

consequence.description: Attaching an incompatible process to a pricing engine causes runtime exceptions during pricing calculations, resulting in backtest failures and strategy abandonment

core.when: When setting up pricing engine for any derivative pricing operation
core.action: Explicitly set evaluation date before any pricing call to verify deterministic time-to-expiry calculations

consequence.description: Without explicit evaluation date setup, the system may use default or stale dates causing incorrect time-to-expiry calculations, leading to wrong option prices and Greeks that do not match live trading systems

core.when: When updating strategy value with changed value or notional value
core.action: update both _values and _notl_values series at the current time index to maintain time series integrity

consequence.description: Missing time series update causes gaps or duplicate values in historical records, corrupting the backtest result time series used for analysis

core.when: When implementing a custom Node subclass
core.action: Implement the update(date, data, inow) method to update the node's state for a given date and data point; if not implemented, must raise NotImplementedError

consequence.description: Strategy will fail with NotImplementedError during backtest execution when the framework attempts to update node state on each simulation date

core.when: When accessing framework capability documentation or marketing materials
core.action: Treat the framework as in alpha stage and expect potential bugs; the framework has not reached production stability and should not be used for live capital management without thorough validation

consequence.description: Users rely on alpha-quality code for live trading without adequate safeguards, causing unexpected behavior, incorrect trades, or system failures during critical trading periods

core.when: When implementing gradient computation for log-likelihood functions in parametric survival models
core.action: Use autograd or equivalent automatic differentiation framework for computing gradients — avoid closed-form derivative implementations that introduce derivation errors and require maintenance for custom distributions

consequence.description: Manual derivative implementations are error-prone and become outdated when distribution log-likelihood functions are modified; backtest results may exhibit numerical instability without automatic differentiation

core.when: When computing numerical delta
core.action: enforce delta bounds: call options must yield values in [0,1] and put options must yield values in [-1,0]

consequence.description: Delta values outside these bounds indicate incorrect finite difference calculation or pricing function errors, leading to incorrect hedging ratios in live trading

core.when: When calculating forward prices across different option pricing models
core.action: Use S*exp(r*t) for Black-Scholes forward and S*exp((r-q)*t) for Black-Scholes-Merton forward, where q is the continuous dividend yield

consequence.description: Forward price mismatch will cause incorrect option pricing — the dividend adjustment in BSM forward formula is essential for accuracy with dividend-paying stocks

core.when: When calculating numerical gamma for options at expiration (t=0)
core.action: Return infinity when spot equals strike (S=K at t=0) and return 0 otherwise — do not use finite difference methods at t=0 as they produce unstable results

consequence.description: Using finite differences at t=0 produces unreliable gamma values that either oscillate wildly or collapse to zero, making same-day expiry risk management impossible and causing strategies to misjudge their gamma exposure near market close

core.when: When implementing stochastic processes or Monte Carlo simulations in option pricing
core.action: Assume random seeds are set consistently across each stochastic components — the framework does not enforce random seed coverage for reproducibility

consequence.description: Without explicit seed control, stochastic results are non-reproducible across runs, making backtesting validation impossible and strategy performance evaluation unreliable

core.when: When processing dividend and split events
core.action: Apply stock splits to interday data (1d, 1wk, 1mo, 3mo) only, not intraday data

consequence.description: Stock split adjustments on intraday data produce incorrect prices because splits are daily-level events

core.when: When repairing Adj Close values
core.action: Assume Adj Close is also corrupted when Close is corrupted, since Yahoo often misadjusts both together

consequence.description: Reconstructing only Close but not Adj Close causes inconsistent price series that breaks backtesting calculations

core.when: When creating or interpolating a cost value
core.action: Allow negative cost numbers

consequence.description: Negative cost values break financial calculations and PnL computations, leading to incorrect balance assertions

core.when: When testing or validating multi-leg sale transactions
core.action: Verify that balanced multi-leg sales (where cash proceeds equal cost basis within tolerance) produce zero validation errors and are accepted by the sellgains plugin

consequence.description: Modifying validation to reject balanced transactions would incorrectly flag valid economic outcomes, causing false accounting errors in correctly structured trades
